races.race_name: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Polemon
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner (1m1f-1m4f) who showed promise in maiden hurdles but his progress stalled in spring 2025, pulling up on handicap hurdle debut and finishing well beaten on his final start for Denis Hogan in May last year; brought down at the first on stable debut after an absence this April (20-1), but still sets the standard with a mark of 110.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner who has shown promise over hurdles; sets the form standard.

runners.name: Yellow Card
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner (AW and turf; 1m1f-1m4f) but form slumped after a promising start for this stable; however, he produced a much-improved effort when runner-up at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) last month on his fourth hurdle start and a repeat of that performance would give him leading claims.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; close second at Ffos Las in May and a repeat gives him leading claims.

runners.name: Keep Off The Sugar
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging start on bumper debut last March, but has gone backwards since and was tailed off on all three subsequent starts; offered little on last month's hurdling debut here (2m1f) after a break, and this longer trip needs to spark a major turnaround.
runners.comment: Promising bumper debut but has struggled since; needs this longer trip to revive fortunes.

runners.name: Brenda Lady
runners.spotlight: Related to several winners, including Dare To Endeavour (useful 2m4f-3m chase); 100-1 when soundly beaten on hurdle debut at Ffos Las (2m, good to soft) a fortnight ago; step up in trip should suit but likely one for longer term.
runners.comment: Related to winners; well beaten on recent hurdle debut and likely one for later on.

runners.name: Tiger Rouge
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two winners, Skatman (useful 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Rockbrook (2m-2m3f hurdle/chase); tailed-off last of seven in a point bumper in March and has plenty to prove on rules debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to winners Skatman and Rockbrook; tailed off in point bumper, much to prove.

runners.name: River Don
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt in points when landing a five-runner maiden in April, but failed to build on that when tailed off and beating only one rival home on last month's hurdle debut over C&D; cheekpieces worn then quickly dispensed with and he needs to leave that effort well behind.
runners.comment: Off the mark in points but well held on hurdle debut; needs big improvement.

runners.name: Premier Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Fair pointer with a 6-15 record (2m4f-3m, good to soft-good to firm: tongue-tied for five) for Bradley Gibbs; arrives on the back of a victory in a small-field mixed open when a short-priced favourite and looks the type to make an impact in this sphere; respected on hurdle/stable debut.
runners.comment: Fair pointer who arrives in winning form and looks the type to do well over hurdles.

runners.name: Tiger Tobin
runners.spotlight: Maiden point winner in February 2024, but remains without further success and has struggled this year; faces a different test on hurdle/stable debut and will need to raise his game considerably.
runners.comment: Maiden point winner in 2024 but has struggled since; plenty to prove on hurdle debut.

runners.name: French Diablo
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 4-1 for his debut at Musselburgh (extended 1m7f, good to soft) in November and travelled with some promise before hanging badly left; failed to back that up when beaten 44l at Newbury the following month but remains open to improvement and this longer trip could suit; hood added.
runners.comment: 4-1 Musselburgh debut promise before hanging left; well held since but still early days.

races.race_name: Edmundson Electrical Torquay Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dunkerque
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick when winning at Kelso (extended 2m5f) ten days ago, making it 2-2 over fences, with all victories coming on good ground (handles soft); had a hard enough race there and shoulders a penalty here, so the booking of 5lb claimer William Shanahan looks a positive and another bold showing can be expected if this run doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Shoulders a penalty, but 2-2 over fences and can go well again with a claimer booked.

runners.name: Doc McCoy
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner but 0-6 over fences; hasn't had much go his way since scoring in October but he was still in contention when unseating three out over fences last time in a race won by Dunkerque; now 10lb better off with that rival and he should be competitive given a change in fortune; forecast rain is a concern though.
runners.comment: Five-time hurdle winner but 0-6 over fences; not out of this but rain a concern.

runners.name: Vengeance
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles and, while there have been hints of encouragement off a sliding mark, he hasn't looked like capitalising and now faces a stiff task from out of the weights on chase debut.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles and faces a stiff task on chase debut from out of the weights.

runners.name: Black Occ
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has inconsistent chase form (P2P3) but looks one of the likelier players provided he backs up latest effort, namely creditable third at Warwick (3m); runs off the same mark.
runners.comment: One of the likelier places provided latest effort (third at Warwick) is backed up
runners.owner: P Conway, T Lewis & G Wilson

runners.name: Daring Plan
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over shorter this year, runner-up at Fontwell (2m5f) last time; one of the main players on recent form but is no certainty to go one better, being 0-23 overall and not a certain stayer back up in trip.
runners.comment: Respected on recent form but is a longstanding maiden and has a stamina doubt
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Isle Of Sark
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdles winner; ran encouragingly on his return to chasing but has failed to back up that effort in two starts since, taking record in this sphere to 0-8; admittedly well treated on best form.
runners.comment: Flat/hurdles winner; 0-8 over fences but is well treated on best form

runners.name: Kenzo Des Bruyeres
runners.spotlight: 0-4 under rules; had a plausible excuse (perhaps unsuited by being ridden much less prominently than usual) on chase debut last time; remains the type to do well in this sphere, being a dual point winner.
runners.comment: Had a plausible excuse on chase debut last time; still the type to do well in this sphere

runners.name: It's A Breeze
runners.spotlight: Maiden who is one of the lesser lights at his major yard and has failed to complete in two starts since switched to fences; on a positive note, reproduction of best hurdles form would give him place claims.
runners.comment: Has failed to complete in two chase starts; best hurdles form gives him place claims

runners.name: Ferando
runners.spotlight: Maiden who is 0-4 over hurdles and fences apiece (2m-2m6f); far from solid on form and chance largely depends on the effects of new trip.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form; new trip needs to help

runners.name: Winning Paddy
runners.spotlight: Record of only 1-11 in points and is far from solid on rules form (three attempts); however, may show the benefit of switching to handicap level off a modest mark.
runners.comment: May show the benefit of switching to handicap level off a modest mark

runners.name: Bee's Kiss
runners.spotlight: Has poor claims on rules form and is 8lb out of weights but may improve for this switch to chasing, being a point winner.
runners.comment: Poor claims on hurdles form but may improve for switch to chasing, being a point winner

runners.name: Jena d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Won in first-time blinkers (retained since) at Taunton (3m, good) in April and had 9l to spare when following up at Stratford (3m2f, good to firm); also effective on soft/good to soft; climbing the weights but leading claims nevertheless.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 in blinkers with 9l Stratford win; climbing the weights but has leading claims

runners.name: Porter In The Park
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Warwick (3m2f, good to soft) in March under regular rider Cameron Johnstone-Baker and she's firmly in the picture on that form but two lesser performances have followed and she needs something extra.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Warwick in March but two lesser runs have followed; needs something extra

runners.name: Queens Venture
runners.spotlight: Completed a double (2m6f/2m5f, good/good to soft) when winning here for Dan Skelton last August; she's run some reasonable races since for this yard but was a well-beaten second of two finishers at Fontwell recently.
runners.comment: Won here last August but well-beaten second of two finishers at Fontwell recently

runners.name: Miss Goldfire
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to win under Ciaran O'Shea at Fontwell (2m5f, good) last month; unseated before the race got serious there last time; stays 3m2f and is effective on good to soft ground; capable of having a big say.
runners.comment: Won at Fontwell last month and unseated before race got serious there last time
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Miss Goldfire

runners.name: Just A Memory
runners.spotlight: Won over fences here (2m5f, good to soft) last September and she returned to form when runner-up in a chase at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) a fortnight ago; 0-6 over hurdles but showed promise in this sphere earlier in her career; could have a part to play if her good form continues.
runners.comment: Runner-up over fences at Ffos Las a fortnight ago; promise over hurdles earlier in career

runners.name: American Land
runners.spotlight: Prolific between November 2024 and June 2025, with his four wins in that spell including two here; his progress seemed to stall subsequently but he ran well when second of five to a progressive winner at Chepstow (2m, good) last time and could be in the mix; effective on good to soft.
runners.comment: Runner-up to a progressive winner at Chepstow last time and he could be in the mix

runners.name: Centara
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive over fences in the first half of last season, winning here four times (2m, good to soft/good); 2m5f here appeared to stretch him last month following some time off; has a lower mark to work with back over hurdles and he could play a leading role back down in trip.
runners.comment: Four chase wins here last year; has a lower mark to work with over hurdles; leading player

runners.name: Arctic Voyage
runners.spotlight: Useful on testing ground on the Flat early in career and this 4yo has tapped back into that promise over hurdles this spring, winning a maiden at Hereford (2m, good) in April on his second stable start and following up in a C&D novice (good); trainer won this with Clearance, after whom this race is named, in 2022 and 2024; intriguing candidate back in a handicap.
runners.comment: 4yo who has impressed on his last two starts; intriguing candidate back in a handicap

runners.name: Howth
runners.spotlight: Switched to hurdles for new yard last May-September and won four of his six starts (all on good ground), two of them over C&D (one a handicap); held in fourth of six at Plumpton last September when last seen but retains potential; yard among the winners and he could go well back from a break.
runners.comment: Prolific when switched to hurdles last May-September; could go well back from a break

runners.name: Morning Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for new stable last season (2m, heavy-good to soft) and he's continued in good form, beaten only 1l when fourth at Fakenham (2m4f, good) last month; the drop back in trip could help; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Three wins last season and he's continued in good form; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Ugo Bingo
runners.spotlight: Has slipped to his last winning mark but he's proving difficult to win with and was well held at Wincanton in April last time; cheekpieces return and, while he copes with most ground, he's likely to be playing for minor honours at best.
runners.comment: Handily weighted but hard to win with and likely playing for minor honours at best.

runners.name: Ambassador
runners.spotlight: Won at Cartmel (2m1f) in August but form has slumped badly since and he has shown no sign of a revival in two runs since returning from a break in April.
runners.comment: Won at Cartmel in August but badly out of form and hard to fancy at present.

runners.name: Get The Value
runners.spotlight: Completed back-to-back wins last spring, including one here, but has been badly out of form since returning from a break in December; now 4lb below his last winning mark, but hard to make a case for in current form.
runners.comment: Dual winner last summer but badly out of form since; hard to fancy despite a good mark.

runners.name: Westerton
runners.spotlight: Built on the promise of his seasonal return when a close second at Warwick (2m, good) last month, despite making a mistake at the last; a 5lb rise asks a little more, though Lewis Stones's 3lb claim helps offset that, and he still looks favourably treated on his best Flat form; with further progress likely, he's high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Up 5lb for close second at Warwick but remains well treated and unexposed; shortlisted.
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

runners.name: Cogital
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest to predict and well held on both starts on the AW since finishing a good second over hurdles at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) in March, ridden by Harry Atkins who can claim his 5lb allowance here; will not mind any rain and not out of this if in the mood, with one of his two hurdling wins coming over C&D.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner; not ruled out if in the mood and rain would be no issue.

runners.name: Marhaba The Champ
runners.spotlight: Largely disappointing for this stable under both codes and, after a brief respite on the Flat at Newbury in April, he refused to come out of the stalls at York last month, both times in this headgear; best hurdle effort when runner-up at Newcastle (2m1f, good) in October offers some hope on handicap switch, but current form is a clear concern.
runners.comment: Hard to warm to in current form but could do better now switched to handicaps.

runners.name: Lord Cauvelliere
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner who failed to transfer that ability in three novice hurdle runs last year, but showed more switched to handicaps at Ludlow (2m, good) last month when keeping on after a mistake at the last; likely has more to offer and is one to consider eased slightly in the weights; unraced on ground worse than good.
runners.comment: More encouragement on handicap debut at Ludlow and he may yet have more to offer.

runners.name: Hawa Jumeirah
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner (1m2f/1m4f); has offered little in three runs over hurdles for this stable but may fare better now switched to handicaps with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; handicap debut and tongue-tie offer hope of improvement.

runners.name: Warfleet
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when runner-up in one of three bumpers in 2024 but he's shown nothing worthwhile since switched to hurdles, finishing tailed off on both runs since returning from an absence in April; makes handicap debut off a lowly mark but with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Bumper promise but shown little over hurdles; makes handicap debut with plenty to prove.

runners.name: Peace Motivator
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for Olly Murphy in 2024 but has failed to complete in all five hurdle starts, including when pulled up on last month's handicap/stable debut; swaps hood for first-time cheekpieces but hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but has failed to complete in five starts over hurdles; hard to recommend.

runners.name: Jukebox Annie
runners.spotlight: Has made little impact since switched to handicaps and, though well supported at Taunton (2m, good) in January, failed to justify the money, struggling a long way out and eventually pulling up; off since and now 14lb lower than her opening mark but can only be watched at present.
runners.comment: Yet to make an impact in handicaps and recent efforts offer little encouragement.

runners.name: Zambezi Fix
runners.spotlight: Has been struggling for form since reappearing in October and offered little encouragement on April's return from just over two months off; has come a long way down in the weights and drops into a Class 5, but that's for good reason and he needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Out of form for a while now but down in the weights and drops in grade.

races.race_name: Visit Our Classic Car Show 6th June Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Matty's Mate
runners.spotlight: £135,000 3yo; brother to winners He Can't Dance (useful bumper/3m hurdle) and Musique De Fee (2m5f-3m3f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to top-class 2m chaser Master Minded and two French Listed winners; represents highly notable connections; respected debutant.
runners.comment: £135,000 3yo; well bred and represents highly notable connections; respected

runners.name: Roccana Rose
runners.spotlight: Dam placed in French 2m3f hurdles, half-sister to winners Honeysuckle (multiple 2m-2m4f Grade 1 hurdle including two Champion Hurdles) and Last Royal (bumper/2m5f hurdle); has had wind surgery; wears tongue-tie and hood on debut; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to Honeysuckle; wears tongue-tie and hood on debut

runners.name: Five Lanes
runners.spotlight: The only contender with bumper form but sets a poor standard, having trailed home last of four at Hereford; ran out in a point prior to that rules debut.
runners.comment: Sets a poor standard, having trailed home last of four at Hereford

runners.name: Ocean Rose
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros foal; dam bumper/2m4f hurdle winner, sister to winners Twelve Roses (useful bumper and 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Cinderella Rose (bumper/2m-2m5f hurdle), out of bumper winner, is granddaughter of Stayers' Hurdle winner Rose Ravine; likely type on breeding.
runners.comment: 10,000euros foal; from a family of bumper/hurdle winners; likely type

runners.name: Megatothemax
runners.spotlight: Closely related to winners Rathhill (useful 2m hurdle) and Vegas Blue (bumper including Listed/2m3f hurdle), half-sister to bumper/hurdle winner Josie Abbing; dam maiden close relative of top-class jumper up to 3m1f Bellshill; stable 1-1 in bumpers this term; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Siblings include two bumper winners; stable 1-1 in bumpers this term; interesting

runners.name: Three Of Spades
runners.spotlight: 5,000gns yearling; sibling to three Flat winners, notably Make Time (Group-placed 7f 2yo/1m; RPR 109); dam 7f winner (87), half-sister to 6f Group 2 winner Overdose; yard not associated with bumper scorers.
runners.comment: 5,000gns yearling; yard not associated with bumper scorers

runners.name: Mr Sunny
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m/9.4f AW winner (RPR 58), out of maiden half-sister to 1m2f-1m4f Flat and useful 2m hurdle winner Middlethorpe; less interesting than stablemate Ocean Rose on breeding.
runners.comment: Less interesting than stablemate Ocean Rose on breeding

runners.name: Thepassingtyphoon
runners.spotlight: Dam runner-up in 2m/2m5f hurdles, out of bumper/1m6f Flat winner; yard has a poor strike-rate in bumpers.
runners.comment: Yard has poor strike-rate in bumpers

runners.name: Winter Flight
runners.spotlight: Rules debutant who isn't particularly compelling on point form; the betting may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Rules debutant who isn't particularly compelling on point form

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: True Charm
runners.spotlight: Showed clear promise with keeping-on second of 12, behind a fellow newcomer, in Windsor maiden (6f, good to firm); the form pick, albeit faces a couple of rivals who could be open to greater progress.
runners.comment: Showed clear promise with second of 12 at Windsor; the form pick

runners.name: Primal
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; Starspangledbanner half-brother to winners Unassuming (1m/8.6f; RPR 99) and Bacchanalian (useful 2m hurdle), out of 1m AW scorer (73); not a pedigree pick but represents major yard.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling; not a pedigree pick but represents major yard

runners.name: Menhaal
runners.spotlight: 260,000gns yearling purchase who is by a very smart 6f performer (including as a 2yo) for his connections; only seventh of ten when 10-11 favourite for Newbury maiden over this trip but showed signs of greenness and may well take a big step forward with the experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Ran below expectations at Newbury but seemed green and may well improve sharply

runners.name: Californian Angel
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling; by Dark Angel and first foal of Listed-placed 6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 102), from family of Lowther/Falmouth stakes winner Nahoodh; stable is operating at a good strike-rate with 2yos this term; possibilities.
runners.comment: 65,000gns yearling; stable is operating at a good strike-rate with 2yos

runners.name: Cilician
runners.spotlight: £90,000 yearling; Blackbeard half-brother to Italian 1m 2yo winner Monte Vista, out of maiden (RPR 63) half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo/7f scorer Kitty Kiernan; suitably bred but stable is 0-7 with 2yos this term.
runners.comment: £90,000 yearling; suitably bred but stable is 0-7 with 2yos this term

runners.name: Tumishi
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns yearling, 180,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Havana Grey half-brother to 6f winner Top Biller (RPR 62); dam 5f scorer (including 2yo; 74), half-sister to 7f-1m2f Group 3 winner Mac Love; the biggest eye-catcher in terms of sales info among the newcomers; interesting.
runners.comment: 180,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Havana Grey; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Hadlan
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling, 38,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Invincible Spirit half-brother to two winners, notably James McHenry (1m; RPR 100); dam 1m4f AW scorer (66), half-sister to 5f/5.5f 2yo Group 2 winner Gutaifan; market helpful.
runners.comment: 38,000gns (breeeze-up) 2yo; Invincible Spirit half-brother to two winners

runners.name: La Fuerza
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling; by Churchill and first foal of French Listed-placed 7f winner (RPR 105) whose siblings include 7f 2yo scorer Blending, from family of Kingman; notable yard's second 2yo runner of the campaign.
runners.comment: 80,000euros yearling; notable yard's second 2yo runner of the campaign

runners.name: Lochryan
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros foal, £85,000 yearling; by Havana Grey; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 74), out of 5f 2yo winner, from family of champion sprinter Lochsong; stable had a winning 2yo newcomer at this course on Sunday; one to bear in mind.
runners.comment: £85,000 yearling; stable had a winning 2yo newcomer here on Sunday

runners.name: Le Grand Etoile
runners.spotlight: Best to forgive his last of nine at Pontefract (6f) where a slipped bit caused him to hang right throughout and prove difficult for his rider to control; better effort should be on the cards.
runners.comment: Slipped bit provides an excuse for his last of nine at Pontefract

runners.name: The Ginger Kid
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros yearling purchase who is from a precocious family; performed well below market expectations (7-2 joint-favourite) in 6f contest at the York Dante festival, finishing down the field, but should be capable of much better; similar type to Menhaal.
runners.comment: Ran below expectations at York but should do better; similar type to Menhaal

runners.name: Trust Yourself
runners.spotlight: Finished last of ten in 6f event at Ascot, living up to market expectations (rank outsider); best watched unless the signals from the betting go the other way.
runners.comment: Last of ten at Ascot, having started the rank outsider

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Miss Tuite
runners.spotlight: Dam placed five times on the AW over 6f; looked green early on her debut at Thirsk (5f, good; 28-1) 18 days ago but then got the hang of things and shaped well, finishing second of ten; that sets the standard but faces some nicely bred newcomers.
runners.comment: Looked green early, then got the hang of things on her debut at Thirsk; sets the standard

runners.name: Fast Track
runners.spotlight: Dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (RPR 97); 16-1 when third of eight on her debut at Goodwood (5f, good) 12 days ago, showing plenty of ability amidst greenness; open to improvement.
runners.comment: 16-1 when third of eight on her debut at Goodwood 12 days ago; open to improvement

runners.name: Senorita Bonita
runners.spotlight: January foal; 900,000gns breeze-up purchase; by Starspangledbanner; first foal; unraced dam closely related to winners Sub Rose (1m4f Group 3) and Astonishing (1m4f Listed), granddaughter of 1m Group 2 winner; was the top lot at this year's Craven breeze-up sale and she's interesting on debut.
runners.comment: By Starspangledbanner; top lot at the Craven breeze-up sale (900,000gns); interesting

runners.name: Halliwell Stream
runners.spotlight: Dam Group-placed 2yo winner (RPR 92); showed some speed but didn't quite see it out when fourth of ten on her debut at Yarmouth (5f, good; 8-1) two weeks ago; open to progress.
runners.comment: Showed some speed but didn't quite see it out on her debut at Yarmouth; open to progress

runners.name: Terminology
runners.spotlight: March foal; 95,000gns foal, 105,000gns yearling, 360,000euros breeze-up 2yo; by Havana Grey; third foal; half-sister to winners Pellitory (7f including 2yo; RPR 102) and Jalaybee (1m AW; 76); dam Listed-placed 5f AW 2yo winner (84); dam won on her debut and there's lots to like on debut for a yard that does well with 2yos.
runners.comment: 360,000euros breeze-up buy; dam Listed-placed 5f AW 2yo winner (84); lots to like on debut

runners.name: Delighfully Hot
runners.spotlight: January foal; filly by Too Darn Hot; second foal; dam 5f AW winner (including 2yo; RPR 85), closely related to 6f 2yo winner El Hadeeyah, out of 6f Listed winning half-sister to Listed-placed winners Rising Talent (1m-1m2f) and Laurentina (6f/7f 2yo); watch the betting on debut.
runners.comment: January foal; by Too Darn Hot; dam 5f AW winner (including 2yo; RPR 85); watch the betting

runners.name: Dreams Galore
runners.spotlight: April foal; by Havana Grey; first foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including Listed; RPR 103), half-sister to winners Romantic Style (7f Group 3) and Town And Country (5f Listed), out of French 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; speedy pedigree and she's one to note in the market on debut.
runners.comment: April foal; by Havana Grey; dam 6f Listed winner on soft; one to note in the market

runners.name: Queen Of Christmas
runners.spotlight: April foal; 305,000euros yearling; by Mehmas; ninth foal; half-sister to five winners including Wasmya (6f/7f including Listed; RPR 106), Wahdan (Group-placed French 9.5f AW; 105) and Talbah (Group-placed 7f; 104); dam 6.5f winner, out of half-sister to Arc winner Dylan Thomas; well related and she's respected on debut for a yard that has won the last three renewals of this.
runners.comment: 305,000euros yearling; half-sister to five winners; yard won last three runnings of this

runners.name: Miss U Gino
runners.spotlight: Showed up well before weakening into fifth of seven on her debut at Newmarket (6f, good; 25-1) 18 days ago; drops in trip with better needed.
runners.comment: Prominent before weakening into fifth on her debut at Newmarket over 6f; drops in trip

runners.name: My Normandie
runners.spotlight: Seventh of ten on debut at Leicester (5f, good to firm) but showed a bit more when fifth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; drop back in trip may not suit and likely she's one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Showed more than on her debut when fifth at Windsor over 6f 11 days ago; back in trip
runners.owner: Gerard A Normand, Anna & Moa Sundstrom

races.race_name: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: Kept on to finish third of ten over this C&D in April and shaped as if still in good form at Windsor (1m, good to firm) next time; disappointed in first-time blinkers here 15 days ago but may bounce back with the headgear removed.
runners.comment: Third over C&D in April; disappointed in blinkers last time but may bounce back

runners.name: Safe Harbor
runners.spotlight: Third of five on her handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) last month and ran well in first-time cheekpieces when second of 13 at Newmarket (1m, good; third has won since) 19 days ago; respected with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Ran well in first-time cheekpieces when second at Newmarket 19 days ago; respected

runners.name: Glasgow Kiss
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in a novice at Southwell on her stable debut in February but she's failed to build on that in three starts since; failed to beat a rival here 25 days ago and now has a visor added.
runners.comment: Promise on her stable debut but hasn't built on that in three starts since; visor on

runners.name: Lillie Margot
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress in novice/maiden events but showed improved form when second of seven over this C&D (good) on her handicap debut 25 days ago; ridden positively that day and she's a contender.
runners.comment: Showed improved form when second over C&D on her handicap debut 25 days ago; contender

runners.name: Mayfair Market
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in four starts last year (6f-1m); failed to make an instant impact in handicaps at Kempton (7f, AW; first-time tongue-tie) in May, having been held up off a steady pace; steps back up in trip.
runners.comment: Failed to make an instant impact in handicaps at Kempton last time; steps back up in trip

runners.name: Vitality
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is unraced on ground slower than good; ran to a similar level on her first two starts but did too much too soon before weakening at Bath (1m, firm) three weeks ago; may bounce back on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ran to a similar level on her first two starts but disappointing last time; handicap debut
runners.owner: Miss D Finkler, A Herd, Dr P Holloway 1

runners.name: Katalyst
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Lingfield (1m, AW) in April but she ran better in first-time blinkers over this C&D (good to soft) 15 days ago; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Ran better in first-time blinkers over this C&D 15 days ago and now needs to build on that

runners.name: Mimi's Magic
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 8l when third of 12 in a maiden at Newcastle (1m, AW) in April and ran with credit when second of four on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) 29 days ago; hung right that day and cheekpieces are added for her first start on turf.
runners.comment: Ran with credit on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f); cheekpieces on; turf debut

runners.name: Laughterintherain
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite when disappointing on her debut at Newmarket in October but it was an improved effort when second of 12 in a novice at Lingfield (7f, AW) in December; ran to a similar level when behind a couple of these on her handicap debut over this C&D in April and she may come on for that first start for 139 days.
runners.comment: Second at Lingfield in December and ran to a similar level on her handicap debut over C&D

runners.name: Bami
runners.spotlight: Won a novice on the AW at Lingfield (7f) in November and posted a solid effort on her handicap debut at Kempton (7f, AW) in December; off since and now steps up in trip with a hood applied on this switch back to turf; broke out of stalls and withdrawn at Lingfield last month (18-1 at the time).
runners.comment: Solid fourth on her handicap debut at Kempton in December; up to 1m with a hood added

runners.name: Morningtoncrescent
runners.spotlight: Sixth in each of her three starts, with the best of those efforts coming at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month; disappointed at Chester (1m4f, good) 26 days ago and now makes her handicap debut over the shortest trip she's attempted in first-time cheekpieces; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Best of her three runs came when sixth at Newbury (1m2f) last month; cheekpieces added
runners.owner: Emma Banks , Rocco, De Ramsey & Dobson

runners.name: Star Time
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) in November 2024 and he was runner-up over that C&D last February; showed he retains ability when fifth at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on his first start for 433 days last month and may come on for it, although this drop back in trip may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Showed he retains ability when fifth at Kempton (1m3f) on his first start for 433 days

runners.name: Something Splendid
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been over 1m2f on good to soft, with the most recent coming at Doncaster in September after the first past the post was disqualified for a banned substance; well beaten at Newbury on his return but this is a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Both wins over 1m2f on good to soft; well beaten in a much higher grade on his return

runners.name: A Major Payne
runners.spotlight: 0-17 but he's finished in the first three on 11 occasions, including in each of his three starts this year; beaten a neck here over 8.5f last summer and he's a solid contender once again.
runners.comment: 0-17 but he's been in the first three on 11 occasions; holds solid claims once again

runners.name: Orangesandlemons
runners.spotlight: Dual winner, with the most recent success coming over 8.5f here in October (good to soft); solid reappearance at Wolverhampton in April but was well held here 25 days ago (trainer's rep said she would prefer an easier surface); steps up in trip and she's a contender if the ground is slower than good.
runners.comment: Course winner in October on good to soft; below her best last time but may bounce back

runners.name: Kasgani
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Ireland for Dermot Weld but he caught the eye on his stable debut over C&D 46 days ago, staying on for third behind Caph Star having been denied a clear run; that was his first start since October and this unexposed gelding is on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Caught the eye when staying on for third on stable debut over C&D in April; shortlisted

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the AW in Class 6 company since December (6f-8.5f); respectable efforts on his last two outings but he possibly didn't quite get home over this trip at Ripon last month; each-way contender.
runners.comment: All four wins on the AW in Class 6 company; possibly didn't quite stay this trip at Ripon

runners.name: Give Me The Night
runners.spotlight: 0-8; placed in a maiden here last June but he's been well held in six starts since, five of which were handicaps; steps up in trip with better needed; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well held since finishing third in a maiden here over 8.5f last June; steps up in trip

runners.name: Caph Star
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground slower than good; off the mark for this yard when winning over C&D in April and he ran another solid race when third of ten at Kempton (1m3f, AW) next time; however, he was reported to have ran flat at Newmarket 19 days ago and needs to bounce back; hood removed.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April but needs to bounce back from a lesser run at Newmarket; hood left off

runners.name: Primo Lara
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Jane Chapple-Hyam but has been quite disappointing since the latest of those wins; held in two starts since undergoing wind surgery and blinkers are now tried instead of cheekpieces; down another 3lb.
runners.comment: Dual winner for previous yard; well held in two starts since wind surgery; blinkers tried

runners.name: Abando
runners.spotlight: Ex-German; well held in two maidens for Marco Botti last year and a similar story in two novice events for this stable; gelded since last seen in February and has cheekpieces back on for his handicap debut, so he's worth keeping an eye on in the betting.
runners.comment: Well held in four starts in Britain; gelded since last seen in February; handicap debut

runners.name: Barbury Boy
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form when winning at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm; runner-up has won twice since) in April but he didn't settle at Haydock (1m4f, good) 25 days ago; this drop back in trip could suit and he's open to further progress.
runners.comment: Won over 1m2f at Beverley in April; didn't settle over 1m4f latest; improve back at 1m2f

runners.name: Gatehouse
runners.spotlight: Won on the AW at Chelmsford (1m2f) in December and followed up on his handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) in April; however, he hung left and was well held on his turf debut at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) 31 days ago; this is a drop in grade.
runners.comment: 2-3 on the AW but hung left before finishing well held on his turf debut at Newmarket

runners.name: Mudita
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on debut at Wolverhampton in March and built on that at Ripon (1m2f, good) 39 days ago, coming clear with the runner-up to win a maiden; that form is ordinary but she should have more to offer in handicaps.
runners.comment: Built on her debut when winning a maiden at Ripon 36 days ago; makes her handicap debut

runners.name: Midnight Rodeo
runners.spotlight: Improved for a step up in trip and switch to nurseries last year, winning at Kempton (1m, AW) and Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in September; 7lb higher than for that latest success and needs to take a step forward from last month's reappearance at Windsor.
runners.comment: Won on the AW at Kempton and Wolverhampton in September; needs to come on for his return
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: That Darn Cantor
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival on his first two starts (1m/1m2f) but showed much more at a huge price when fifth of nine at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) 34 days ago; gelded since and this step back up in trip should suit on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed more when 6th at Yarmouth (1m) latest; gelded since; up in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Unchartedterritory
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in novice company but no immediate impact in handicaps when down the field at Hamilton (8.5f, good) 19 days ago; raced freely that day and will need to settle better over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Raced freely when down the field at Hamilton (8.5f) on handicap debut; steps up in trip

runners.name: Hint Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the AW over 7f in February and has gone close on his last two starts, including over this trip 28 days ago; has form on turf and this prominent-racer has claims.
runners.comment: Won twice on the AW over 7f in February and ran well on last two starts; has form on turf

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been on the AW over further than this, the latest at Wolverhampton in January; below his best in three starts since finishing third there in February and he's 0-15 on turf.
runners.comment: All five wins on the AW over further and below his best on his last three starts

runners.name: Fancy Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won three times for Ollie Sangster (5.5f/6f); solid effort when beaten only 2l at Wolverhampton on her stable debut in December but she's not built on that in two starts since; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Solid effort on her stable debut but hasn't built on that in two starts since; tongue-tied

runners.name: Jake Loves Laura
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but ran one of her better races for this yard when a 2l third of seven at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in February; however, she was disappointing in a classified event 95 days ago and has been off since.
runners.comment: 0-15; off since disappointing in a classified event at Lingfield 95 days ago; back on turf

runners.name: Run This Way
runners.spotlight: Had blinkers on for the first time when winning at Redcar (6f, good; has won on soft) last month and followed up at Thirsk (6f, good) 18 days ago, beating 16 rivals; back up another 7lb but can't be dismissed in her bid for the hat-trick.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 in blinkers when winning at Thirsk last time; back up 7lb but not dismissed

runners.name: Rinky Tinky Tinky
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on stable debut last April (good to firm) but quiet at the end of 2025; fair fifth of 12 at Bath (5.5f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces replaced blinkers) on reappearance; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: C&D winner on stable debut last April; quiet end to year; fair reappearance at Bath latest

runners.name: May Encounter
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but she has posted some respectable efforts, including when fifth of eight at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on her reappearance last month; dropped another 2lb.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden but has posted some respectable efforts; dropped 2lb since Windsor fifth

runners.name: Ishe Worth Agamble
runners.spotlight: Won three times last season, the most recent coming at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) in July; respectable third over this C&D on his return in April and kept on for fifth of 11 at Redcar (6f, good) 16 days ago; contender if things drop right.
runners.comment: Won three times in 2025; returned in form; usually held up so needs things to drop right

runners.name: Tilsworth Turf
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over 6f on soft ground in 2024 but he's mostly struggled since the latest of them; off for 134 days and he's best watched.
runners.comment: Both wins over 6f on soft ground but he's mostly struggled since the latest of those

runners.name: Surprise Again
runners.spotlight: Poor form in five starts, including in two handicaps (6f/7f); absent for 254 days and much better is needed.
runners.comment: Poor form in five starts, including in two handicaps; off for 254 days

runners.name: Alafdhal
runners.spotlight: Last five wins have been over 5f, the most recent at Wolverhampton (AW) in December; below best in four starts since and now returns to turf for the first time since October 2024 folllowing a 113-day break.
runners.comment: Below his best in four starts since his Wolverhampton win in December; off for 113 days

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Has shown his best form at Dundalk and needs to improve on this season's turf form; best placing on turf was in a 1m claimer at Gowran; big price when in rear over 7f here 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Has shown his best form at Dundalk and needs to improve on this season's turf form
runners.owner: Total Recall Racing Club & J Fairhurst & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Boston Max
runners.spotlight: Three undistinguished runs on turf last season; different proposition this term since being gelded; won over 5f at Dundalk on AW/ handicap/seasonal debut in first-time tongue-tie; got up late to share the honours over the same trip at Chepstow 13 days ago; may find further improvement at this distance.
runners.comment: Trip can help him to continue on the upgrade after AW win and Chepstow dead-heat at 5f

runners.name: Whistling Jamesie
runners.spotlight: Won at 20-1 on handicap debut over 6f at Dundalk last October; in rear over 7f at the same course in April; last of 12 at Naas; blinkers tried now; seems risky.
runners.comment: 20-1 winner at Dundalk last autumn, risk after finishing last in two races this year

runners.name: Gonna Be Golden
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; Dundalk maiden winner last December (7f); heavy ground probably against him when tailed off on seasonal/handicap debut over 1m at this venue; saddle slipped early in 6f Fairyhouse contest last week; the run can be ignored
runners.comment: 7f winner on AW, ran poorly on heavy ground on seasonal debut, saddle slipped on latest

runners.name: Cisterna
runners.spotlight: Uneven form last season; has improved gradually this season; stayed well over an extended 7f to open his account at Roscommon; possibly vulnerable over this trip off 5lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Winner over an extended 7f last time, possibly vulnerable over this trip off 5lb higher

runners.name: Kilmac Air
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame twice at Cork; opening mark seems fair and she may improve on better ground with cheekpieces fitted; 5lb claim will help too.
runners.comment: Opening mark seems fair and she may improve on better ground with cheekpieces fitted
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs M V Magnier

runners.name: Schoolyard Days
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form at sprint trips; failed to improve when raised on distance and equipped with cheekpieces in a claimer at Roscommon.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form at sprint trips, failed to improve when raised in distance in a claimer

runners.name: Aurora Mist
runners.spotlight: Obtained plenty of experience at two; won over 1m at Gowran on second start this season; stable may have a stronger candidate in another last-time-out winner Cisterna
runners.comment: Won over 1m at Gowran, stable may have a stronger candidate in Cisterna

runners.name: Pocket Jacks
runners.spotlight: Midfield in a pair of Dundalk maidens (6f/7f) at two;  hinted at possible improvement over 5f on reappearance at Navan; may slot into handicaps effectively.
runners.comment: Hinted at improvement over 5f at Navan last month, may slot into handicaps effectively

runners.name: Windsor Lily
runners.spotlight: Not beaten fair in fifth in a 6f Dundalk maiden in February; no impact over 7f on soft at Gowran in her first handicap, her chance is not obvious.
runners.comment: No impact over 7f on soft at Gowran in her first handicap, her chance is not obvious
runners.owner: Shane R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Enthusiastically
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk nursery win on AW debut last November; confirmed a fair standard of form with two fourth placings later that month; formerly trained by Donnacha O'Brien; did not quite see out the 1m trip when making a satisfactory debut for Sean Hayes at Dundalk in March; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Good chance based on solid Dundalk form, appeared not to stay over 1m last time

runners.name: Apothic Red
runners.spotlight: Ignored in the betting on handicap debut in a 6f event here in April; kept on to finish sixth of 21, showing enough to suggest an each-way chance now.
runners.comment: Sixth of 21 on handicap debut at this venue, showed enough to suggest an each-way chance
runners.owner: Mrs Pauline McCourt/Donal O'Leary/Don't Ask The Wife Synd

runners.name: Amerilis
runners.spotlight: Ran twice for Paddy Twomey at two; made a good start for Gillian Scott when fourth of 12 in a 6f maiden here  in March, running on well in the closing stages; not beaten far in mid-field here on handicap debut; could go well at a decent price.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in mid-field here on handicap debut, could go well at a decent price

runners.name: Caitouna
runners.spotlight: Two respectable Dundalk runs; fair effort at Leopardstown on turf debut; useful 7lb claimed; not without a chance.
runners.comment: Has shown just about enough in maidens to suggest a fair chance, useful 7lb claimed

runners.name: Dawn Flame
runners.spotlight: Dundalk win in January (1m) seems to have been a flash in the pan; started his career with two poor runs on turf.
runners.comment: Dundalk win in January (1m) seems to have been a flash in the pan, plenty to prove now

runners.name: Gosford Queen
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort when fifth of nine over 5f at Dundalk on the second of three starts as a juvenile; open to improvement, but others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: The second of her three runs at two offers some hope, others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Never threatened in three maidens at 6f-1m on soft/AW; got going late when sixth of 13 on handicap debut over an extended 1m1f; a great deal better when winning over 7f at Down Royal last Friday; an improving filly but may find a few too good over this trip under a 7lb penalty
runners.comment: 7f Down Royal winner last week; may find a few too good over this trip under a 7lb penalty

runners.name: Polanco
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens last year; 50-1, in rear at Naas on handicap debut.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in maidens last year, 50-1 in rear at Naas on handicap debut

runners.name: Dubai Opulence
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three maiden runs on soft or heavy ground; hard to make a case for him.
runners.comment: Modest form in three maiden runs on soft or heavy ground, hard to make a case for him
runners.owner: Syndicates Racing/W Jones/N O'Hare/Kabin Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Whats It All About
runners.spotlight: Weak juvenile maiden form; failed to feature over 5f at Dundalk in her first handicap; needs to show some spark before becoming a betting proposition.
runners.comment: Failed to feature over 5f at Dundalk in her first handicap, needs to show some spark

runners.name: Wild Berries
runners.spotlight: Best of her three efforts in maidens was over 7f at Naas; well beaten over 1m in a nursery at the same venue; no recent outing; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a 1m nursery at Naas last September, no recent outing, others preferred

runners.name: The Piper's Call
runners.spotlight: Decent level of form in two runs last summer at 6f (both on good); second of 13 behind subsequent Group 3 runner-up Flushing Meadows at this venue; not quite as good but respectable nonetheless when third at Naas; gelded since.
runners.comment: Showed enough in two runs at two to suggest a leading chance in this company

runners.name: Quinta Girl
runners.spotlight: Best effort at two when a close fourth in a 6f Naas maiden; flopped in a Dundalk nursery, leaving her with a question to answer on reappearance.
runners.comment: Fair effort at Naas before a nursery flop at Dundalk that represents a blot on her record
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Sancerre Rock
runners.spotlight: Made late headway when sixth of 10 at Limerick on debut; beaten further at Gowran; will need to qualify for a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Opening runs at Limerick and Gowran imply he will need to qualify for a handicap mark

runners.name: Finika
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-sister to winners Kodiak West (Spanish/US 1m/1m1f; RPR 103), Fregada (6f 2yo; 97), Rayat (6f/8.6f inc AW; 91) and Angel Grey (6f 2yo; 83); dam German 1m winner, out of 6f/7f winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Carrizo Creek.
runners.comment: Dam German 1m winner, out of a winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Carrizo Creek

runners.name: Aestus Astevias
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to winners Jakajaro (Listed-placed 5f/6f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 109) and Stand Strong (2m AW; 80); unraced dam closely related to UAE Derby winner Khawlah (dam of Derby winner Masar), half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Vancouverite
runners.comment: Unraced dam closely related to UAE Derby winner Khawlah, the dam of Derby winner Masar

runners.name: Lady Moon
runners.spotlight: E24,000 Y; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Imperial Sands (1m AW; RPR 106) and The Falling Man (Italian 7f/7.5f AW inc 2yo); dam well held 9.5f in France only start, half-sister to 1m3f-1m5f winner Heart Of Soul.
runners.comment: E24,000 yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to two winners, dam ran only once
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Ask Me Again
runners.spotlight: Failed to sell for E240,000 as a yearling; eighth foal; closely related to US Grade 3 turf winner Queen Picasso; half-brother to four winners including Aclaim (6f-1m inc 2yo/AW/Group 1; 118) and Aimeric (Listed-placed 11.6f-1m5f; 114); dam 7f winner.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Prix de la Foret winner Aclaim, failed to sell for E240,000 as a yearling

runners.name: Tide Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Plenty of merit in a debut run that saw him finish eighth of 24 at this venue; left an impression that he can improve.
runners.comment: Left an impression that he can improve from his debut eighth of 24 at this venue

runners.name: Trek Home
runners.spotlight: Likely to be capable of building on last month's debut fourth in a 5f maiden at Navan
runners.comment: Likely to be capable of building on last month's debut fourth in a 5f maiden at Navan
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Contrary To Law
runners.spotlight: Ran twice at two; not disgraced at 100-1 when ninth of 15 over 7f at this venue; too keen at Galway when fourth of eight behind  Constitution River; looked a possible future winner when keeping fourth in a 16-runner 6f maiden here in March on seasonal/ stable debut.
runners.comment: Looked a possible future winner when keeping fourth in a 16-runner 6f maiden here in March

runners.name: Starborn Legend
runners.spotlight: E29,000 Y, E330,000 Breeze Up purchase; 12th foal; brother to Italian 5f/6f winner Scalea, half-brother to four winners inc String Theory (1m-9.4f AW; RPR 106) and I Could Do Better (6.7f-1m3f inc 2yo/Australia; 96); dam unraced half-sister to two Group 1 winners.
runners.comment: E330,000 Breeze Up purchase, dam an unraced half-sister to two Group 1 winners

runners.name: Forbidden
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 69), half-sister to useful 7f/1m winner Selinka (dam of 5f Group 3 winner Hit The Bid), out of 6f Listed winner; best watched on belated debut at four
runners.comment: Dam a 6f winner, plenty of speed in the pedigree, best watched on belated debut at four

runners.name: Frosted Sky
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never in contention first time out at Dundalk; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Slowly away and never in contention first time out at Dundalk, can be ruled out

runners.name: Highwayman
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never in contention on debut at Cork; likely to need more experience.
runners.comment: Slowly away and never in contention on debut at Cork, likely to need more experience

runners.name: Kula Bula Boyo
runners.spotlight: Faded after a prominent run in a 7f maiden at Cork; needs to find considerable improvement; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Needs to find considerable improvement from a debut run at Cork. cheekpieces fitted

runners.name: Little Roy
runners.spotlight: Betrayed inexperience first time at Sligo; can be left out of calculations.
runners.comment: Betrayed inexperience first time at Sligo; can be left out of calculations

runners.name: Niobite
runners.spotlight: Always at the back on debut over 1m2f here, takes a big drop in trip with a tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Always at the back on debut over 1m2f here, takes a big drop in trip with a tongue-tie on

runners.name: Pick A Window
runners.spotlight: No sign of worthwhile form in three outings last season, struggled again at Cork on return.
runners.comment: No sign of worthwhile form in three outings last season, struggled again at Cork on return

races.race_name: Sky Bet Club Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Bull Shark
runners.spotlight: Third all three starts, the first two at this venue, over 5f on heavy and 6f on soft; beaten almost 8l behind the subsequent Marble Hill winner Great Barrier Reef on the second occasion; beaten almost as far behind the smart-looking Confucius at Naas last time; his experience should be an asset but he needs to raise his game to win a maiden.
runners.comment: His experience should be an asset but he needs to raise his game to win a maiden

runners.name: Giant Sequoia
runners.spotlight: Third foal; by Frankel out of a Listed-winning daughter of Camelot; dam is a sister to Irish Derby winner Latrobe, half-sister to Group 3 winner Diamond Fields; Ryan Moore aboard for debut with Wayne Lordan on Aix La Chapelle.
runners.comment: By Frankel out of a Listed-winning daughter of Camelot, Ryan Moore aboard for debut

runners.name: Aix La Chapelle
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; Justify colt is a brother to Statuette, who won a 6f Group 2 race here as a juvenile on her second and final start, closely related to Group 1 winner Tenebrism, half-brother to Classic winner Henri Matisse, and two other winners; dam a dual Group 1 winner; superb pedigree, Ryan Moore on Giant Sequoia.
runners.comment: Brother to Group 2 winner Statuette. Group 1-winning dam has produced two Group 1 winners

runners.name: Breath Of Paradise
runners.spotlight: E120,000 Breeze Up purchase; Blackbeard colt is the first foal out of a mare who ran only twice; solid American pedigree.
runners.comment: Blackbeard colt, first foal out of a mare who ran only twice, solid American pedigree

runners.name: Peckedbytheparrott
runners.spotlight: E40,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Solomon Islands; unraced dam out of a 1m2f Listed winner.
runners.comment: half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Solomon Islands; unraced dam out of a 1m2f Listed winner

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: 360,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to the useful Make Me King (four wins in stakes races) and juvenile scorer Nardra; dam a Listed winner by Rock Of Gibraltar, and a half-sister to Prix Morny winner The Wow Signal.
runners.comment: Half-brother to the useful Make Me King, dam a half-sister to a Prix Morny winner

runners.name: Selwyn
runners.spotlight: Finished 1 1/2l behind Bull Shark when fourth at Naas on debut; may improve enough to reverse form with that rival who was having his third start.
runners.comment: May improve enough to reverse Naas form with Bull Shark who was having his third start

runners.name: Sirocco Sands
runners.spotlight: Stayed on for second in a 5f maiden at Cork, looks best of the four who have run; probably vulnerable here with a few interesting newcomers in the line-up.
runners.comment: Stayed on for second in a 5f maiden at Cork, looks best of the four who have run

runners.name: Little Miss Muffet
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 7f 2yo winner (inc Listed; RPR 113), sister to useful middle-distance winner Wisconsin, half-sister to winners Willow (Listed) and Sir John Hawkins, out of Irish Oaks/Yorkshire Oaks winner Peeping Fawn; may need further than this.
runners.comment: Dam a 7f Listed winner and a sister to useful winner Wisconsin, may need further than this
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Captain James Cook
runners.spotlight: 190,000gns foal, 500,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to Group 1-winning sprinter Arizona Blaze, and two other winners; dam, by top-class sprinter Equiano, was a 1m2f AW winner at a modest level.
runners.comment: 500,000gns yearling, half-brother to Group 1 winner Arizona Blaze, obvious contender

runners.name: Tradewinds
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten fifth on debut at Navan; the runner-up The Scallionator won well at Listowel on Sunday, but this one left an impression that he may need further than this.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten fifth on debut at Navan, the runner-up won well at Listowel on Sunday

runners.name: Winnie's Wish
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when last of eight behind the promising Sergei Diaghilev over C&D on debut; unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: 20-1 when last of eight behind the promising Sergei Diaghilev over C&D on debut

runners.name: Orchestrating
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to six winners including Group 3 scorer Efaadah and Alrahma; dam a Listed winner by Oasis Dream; blinkers declared for debut; Ryan Moore on Captain James Cook.
runners.comment: Half-brother to six winners including Group 3 scorers Efaadah and Alrahma, wears blinkers
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Thatsanotherstory
runners.spotlight: £17,000 Y; third foal; half-sister to half-sister to two winners; dam won over 5f at two, may need experience.
runners.comment: Third foal; half-sister to two winners, dam won over 5f at two, may need experience

runners.name: One Number
runners.spotlight: $55,000 yearling, $140,000 2yo; third foal; half-brother to US 6.5f/1m dirt winner Penei; unraced dam out of US 1m/1m1f winner; interesting newcomer for in-form top stable.
runners.comment: $140,000 buy this year; third foal; half-brother to a US dirt winner, deserves respect

runners.name: La Tache
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling; fifth foal; brother to 5f AW winner Lady Nunthorpe, half-brother to two winners, notably Group-placed Walbank; dam 5f 2yo winner, half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Albigna (Prix Marcel Boussac for Jessica Harrington).
runners.comment: 140,000gns yearling, dam won over 5f and is a half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Albigna

runners.name: Dropping Dimes
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to three minor winners, dam 1m5f AW winner; stable not known for juvenile winners.
runners.comment: Eighth foal; half-sister to three minor winners, dam 1m5f AW winner, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Gwen
runners.spotlight: E12,000 yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to Flat/hurdle winner Dumb Love; dam 1m AW winner; faces a tough task here first time out.
runners.comment: Relatively cheap yearling purchase, faces a tough task here first time out

runners.name: Celeron
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; third foal; closely related to a winner in France and Greece; dam an Italian 6f Listed winner, half-sister to an Italian Group-placed winner; relatively cheap purchase, has a decent sprinting pedigree and is in good hands.
runners.comment: Relatively cheap purchase, has a decent sprinting pedigree and is in good hands

runners.name: Tennesspeed
runners.spotlight: $85,000 yearling, 200,000gns Craven Breeze Up purchase; fourth foal; half-brother to a pair of US dirt winners; dam unraced half-sister to useful US sprint winner; noteworthy but might not be suited by any significant ease in the ground.
runners.comment: 200,000gns Breeze Up purchase, fourth foal, half-brother to two US winners, noteworthy

runners.name: Nation Blaze
runners.spotlight: 10-1 on debut in a 5f maiden at Cork a month ago; never showed and finished ninth of ten; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: 10-1 on debut in a 5f maiden at Cork a month ago, never showed and finished ninth of ten

races.race_name: Sky Bet Price Boosts Premier Handicap

runners.name: Allsortz
runners.spotlight: Progressive front-runner won Dundalk maiden, then finished third on handicap debut behind Amiata (6f, Naas) before reversing form with that rival back at today's trip at Cork last month, seeing it out well on soft; 5lb worse off with Amiata but every chance of confirming form and won't mind rain.
runners.comment: Progressive front-runner beat two of these at Cork last month; big player, won't mind rain

runners.name: Bad Boy Rizz
runners.spotlight: Navan 2yo maiden winner has returned with two good runs in handicaps, chasing home Amiata at Naas (6f, good) and never quite on terms third to front-running Allsortz at Cork (soft); stiffer 5f should suit.
runners.comment: Never quite on terms third to front-running Allsortz at Cork (soft); stiffer 5f to suit

runners.name: Cool Azul
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced gelding posted good second on debut in 6f maiden here in April (soft), not quite as good but solid runs both starts since, hanging left at Cork and plenty of speed dropped to 5f at Dundalk last week when third in ordinary maiden; respected on handicap debut, rain would suit.
runners.comment: Good debut run here; respected on handicap debut, rain would suit

runners.name: Amiata
runners.spotlight: 5f Navan maiden winner and gained first handicap win at Naas in April (6f, good), beating Bad Boy Rizz 1.5l; solid Cork effort off 7lb higher when fourth to all-the-way winner Allsortz over this trip (soft); considered.
runners.comment: Recent Cork fourth to Allsortz closely matched on revised terms

runners.name: Sparky Sparky
runners.spotlight: Standout run on seasonal return when saying on third behind a smart type in Cork maiden (5f, soft) in April; ran no sort of race there last month before just fair fourth on Dundalk AW last week (3l off Cool Azul) when tried in a tongue-tie; nicely-weighted on best form and rider's claim a bonus on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Nicely-weighted on best form and rider's claim a bonus on handicap debut

runners.name: Ipanema Queen
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden here last year but seemed better suited to 5f as the season progressed, winning Listed races here and at Dundalk; below best when favourite both starts this year, beaten a nose by a stablemate at Dundalk (6f) and dropped out tamely in Cork fillies Listed last month on soft; return to conventional 5f to suit though may not want too much rain on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Below best twice this year; back to best trip but vulnerable giving away lots of weight
runners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Fox In Flight
runners.spotlight: Dundalk maiden win came at this trip over the winter, later third to Sovereign Cry in conditions event at same track; soft ground may not have suited on last month's return when last of eight behind stablemate Allsortz on handicap debut at Cork; hard to see him reversing Cork form with several of these.
runners.comment: Soft ground may not have suited on last month's return; hard to see him reversing form

runners.name: Tornado Kiss
runners.spotlight: Beat Bad Boy Rizz in Cork 2yo maiden but twice safely held in Listed company, most recently didn't give his true running (upset in stalls) when never sighted behind Allsortz on handicap debut at Cork (soft); bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Below best both starts this term, upset in stalls behind Allsortz at Cork latest

runners.name: Real Encounter
runners.spotlight: Narrowly beaten by Allsortz in Dundalk maiden in April before winning over 5f there 13 days later; definite merit in handicap debut at Naas last month, close up until reportedly hitting a ridge over a furlong down before rallying; could be given another chance.
runners.comment: Okay handicap debut at Naas where excuses; remains of interest

runners.name: Sovereign Cry
runners.spotlight: Progressive profile on the AW last season but too keen when soundly beaten on turf/seasonal debut at Naas last month; better back at Dundalk since but doubts remain back on turf, especially if ground eases.
runners.comment: Best form on AW, sole turf run was poor; may not want much rain
runners.owner: John B O'Hagan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Focaccia
runners.spotlight: Promising debut third in 6f maiden here in March; out of depth in Listed class next time, recent AW second (Cool Azul third) was her best effort yet; tailflasher; cheekpieces now tried on handicap debut (3lb wrong).
runners.comment: Tailflasher but recent AW second puts her in the mix; cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Shadow Run
runners.spotlight: Definite step in the right direction on seasonal return at Naas in March when beaten a neck (every chance) on soft but didn't get home on similar ground in Cork rated event following month; blinkers replaced by visor here; 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Promising seasonal return at Naas wasn't backed up at Cork; visor on now; 4lb wrong

races.race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Handicap

runners.name: Emit
runners.spotlight: Consistent last season, going close in 1m2f Group 3 Gallinule Stakes here and later in year twice runner-up in Longchamp Group contests over 1m7f; first two runs this term at 1m6f were creditable, drop back 1m4f didn't suit latest; drops to handicap company and back to ideal trip.
runners.comment: 1m4f didn't suit latest; drops to handicap company and back to ideal trip

runners.name: Factual Fact
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner has done well since returning to the Flat, second here over 1m4f and handled better ground to win 2m1f Killarney amateur handicap last month under a positive ride; 4lb higher but could have more to offer off this low weight upped in grade.
runners.comment: Killarney winner up 4lb but could have more to offer off this low weight upped in grade

runners.name: Yulia
runners.spotlight: Two wins in France (on soft and AW); has won over 1m7f, and had never raced at under 1m4f until recent Irish debut at Sligo; unplaced favourite after starting slowly and meeting trouble in running (1m2f); returns to a more suitable trip.
runners.comment: Inadequate trip on last month's yard debut; this trip to suit

runners.name: Tribal Star
runners.spotlight: Seen mostly on AW, including fourth of 20 in the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m) last June; running well on AW this year and good effort back on turf at Chester last month, only fading late on over 2m3f; upped in grade here and could go well with rider's claim.
runners.comment: Chester fourth upped in grade and could go well with rider's claim

runners.name: Granite Bay
runners.spotlight: Showed good form in 2024 with a trio of wins from 1m2f-1m6f and runner-up in three of last four starts last term; plenty to take from seasonal return at Gowran in April when fourth to Holy See (1m4f, soft), rallying again late on; one to consider.
runners.comment: Gowran comeback fourth to Holy See augurs well; one to consider

runners.name: Dawn Rising
runners.spotlight: No wins last season but strong form in defeat in big staying handicaps; fourth in the amateur handicap at Galway, fifth in the Irish Cesarewitch and signed off with a fine second in the Cesarewitch; recent run here was over an inadequate trip, more expected of here and seemingly McMonagle's pick.
runners.comment: Recent run here was over an inadequate trip, more expected of here back up in trip

runners.name: Holy See
runners.spotlight: Useful AW winner in France over 1m/1m1f; campaigned mainly over hurdles by current yard, winning Limerick novice on heavy over Christmas; fine first effort on the Flat for current yard when winning 1m4f Gowran handicap under today's rider in April (soft) under a positive ride; 5lb higher here but remains of interest.
runners.comment: Gowran winner 5lb higher here but remains of interest

runners.name: Perry Mason
runners.spotlight: Was recording a third win at this track when scoring over 1m2f last month (good), making virtually all and just holding on; previously ran well over 1m4f, this his first attempt at this longer trip.
runners.comment: Recent 1m2f winner here; this his first attempt at this longer trip

runners.name: Poetic Sound
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m4f in 2024 and began last season in form of his life over 1m6f at Navan, touched off in handicap and fine third in Listed contest; last month's 1m4f Leopardstown comeback run was encouraging, could well improve enough from that to play a major role here back at 1m6f.
runners.comment: Leopardstown comeback run encouraging, could well improve enough to play major role

runners.name: Too Bossy For Us
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat, although sole win came back in 2024, narrowly beaten here over 2m last summer; equally talented hurdler, but some stiff tasks of late; market likely informative back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Narrowly beaten here over 2m last summer; back from hurdling, market should guide

runners.name: Tyson Fury
runners.spotlight: Wins have been at up to 2m (AW), winning claimer at Dundalk in March too; runner-up in ordinary maiden hurdles the last twice; can't be ruled out back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Runner-up in ordinary maiden hurdles the last twice; can't be ruled out back on the Flat

runners.name: En Or
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner but losing run goes back to December 2024; hurdling last winter without success, may need this comeback run.
runners.comment: Hurdling last winter without success, may need this comeback run

races.race_name: Try Racing TV For Free Now At racingtv.com/freetrial Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Arrietty
runners.spotlight: Has had plenty of chances, runner-up on four of six maiden runs over shorter trips, most recently on turf return when clear second over 1m2f at Naas in late April; good chance she'll get this trip (Irish Oaks entry) but will need to improve for it.
runners.comment: Seemingly exposed filly but longer trip could bring more

runners.name: I Hope You Dance
runners.spotlight: Improvement on 2yo form when second over this trip on both starts this spring, narrowly beaten after having every chance at Dundalk and made much of running until outstayed by an 86-rated rival at Cork in testing conditions; should go well again.
runners.comment: Runner-up both starts this term at this trip; should go well again

runners.name: Pleased
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns Y; sixth foal; half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Fast Attack (inc AW/Group 3; RPR 104); dam 5f/6f winner (inc triple Listed; 109), from speedy family; encouraging Limerick debut in late April over this trip, never landing a blow in finishing third to an odds-on stablemate; improvement expected.
runners.comment: Encouraging Limerick debut and more expected here

runners.name: Porters Corner
runners.spotlight: Galiway filly cost 85,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to three winners, notably Jo Pickett (French 1m2f-1m4f inc Listed; RPR 98) and Balakovna (French 1m-1m2f); dam placed 9.7f in France, half-sister to Italian 1m Group 1 winner Red Dubawi; Irish Oaks entry has to be noted if strong in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Irish Oaks entry has to be noted if strong in the market on debut

runners.name: Solas Na Gealai
runners.spotlight: Teofilo filly fetched a meagre 2,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Daylight Come (9.4f/13.6f; RPR 90) and Sli Na Ghealai (7f 2yo; 85); dam unraced sister to Irish Derby winner Soldier Of Fortune; market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Cheaply bought Teofilo filly; market best guide on debut

runners.name: Possum
runners.spotlight: Australia half-sister to 10.6f AW/1m4f turf winner Elsa's Pride (RPR 67); dam 9.4f AW/1m4f turf winner (96), closely related to useful 1m4f/1m6f winner Flaming Marvel; another newcomer for whom market likely best guide.
runners.comment: Australia filly another newcomer for whom market likely best guide

runners.name: Kashala
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly is second foal of a 9.7f-2m winner (inc Group 3; RPR 110), half-sister to Listed-placed 1m winner Kassaba, out of half-sister to 1m6f Group 1 winner Kastoria; nicely-bred newcomer has to be noted for market support.
runners.comment: Nicely-bred newcomer has to be noted for market support
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Miss Ivor
runners.spotlight: Modest level of form over 6f/7f; dramatic jump in trip here with cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Modest level of form over 6f/7f; dramatic jump in trip here with cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Ashikita
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 73) half-sister to winners Ashraf (useful 1m/9.4f) and Ashqar (2m hurdle); absent since finishing last of 13 on debut for Dermot Weld in Dundalk AW maiden over two years ago so likely best watched.
runners.comment: Absent since AW debut run over two years ago; seems best watched

races.race_name: Sky Bet Build A Bet Handicap

runners.name: Carrigans Grove
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a successful 2025, winning three times, including over C&D; both runs this term have been here, more encouraging effort latest when not quite staying 7f trip; just 2lb above winning C&D mark last summer.
runners.comment: 7f a bit latest, prospects off just 2lb above winning C&D mark last summer

runners.name: Jazzit
runners.spotlight: Ten race maiden shaped well on AW in April and latest of two turf runs offered some encouragement; visor now replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Latest of two turf runs offered some encouragement; visor now replaces cheekpieces

runners.name: Irish Rumour
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 29 starts; went close on occasions last year (5f-7f), far too keen on seasonal return at Roscommon last month; champion jockey stays loyal so more expected of.
runners.comment: Hard to win with but down to career-low mark and McMonagle stays loyal

runners.name: Angel Of Promise
runners.spotlight: Handicap debut over C&D in April was poor; Keane booked now, so maybe not the forlorn hope she seems.
runners.comment: Handicap debut over C&D in April was poor; Keane booked so maybe not a forlorn hope

runners.name: Four Blondes
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 20 starts; runner-up five times; encouraging C&D comeback fourth last month puts her in the mix although has a bit to find with Hugo's Girl on that run.
runners.comment: Not a great strike rate but encouraging C&D comeback fourth last month puts her in the mix

runners.name: Shamanka
runners.spotlight: Filly's seven runs for Natalia Lupini all on Dundalk AW, well held on handicap debut when last seen in November; tongue-tied for yard debut and seems best watched on turf debut.
runners.comment: All previous run on AW; long absent and best watched on turf debut

runners.name: Hugo's Girl
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden has run some solid races in defeat, notably last month over C&D when staying on third of 20 after a slow start (good); on a fair mark, could go well given a level break.
runners.comment: Recent C&D third could go well given a level break

runners.name: Gegenpressing
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in four runs at Sligo, most recently last month (third has won since) at 6.5f; 4lb rise okay but record at this track relatively underwhelming so needs to improve that.
runners.comment: Sligo specialist, winning there last month; not nearly as good here

runners.name: Jon Riggens
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner over 5f/6f on varying ground; most recent run this term was a step in the right direction (considerately-handled never nearer sixth) and could reverse form with a couple of these with top rider replacing inexperienced 10lb claimer; down to a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Most recent run this term was a step in the right direction and down to a career-low mark

runners.name: Hot To Foxtrot
runners.spotlight: Maiden competitive in 7f/6f handicaps on the AW this season but needs to find more on turf return; cheekpieces replace tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Better AW form, needs to find more on turf return; cheekpieces replace tongue-tie

runners.name: Kitty Bear
runners.spotlight: Improver this year, all three wins under this rider, most recently over extended 7f at Roscommon last month, coming home strongly after being switched to the outer in straight; hit with a 10lb rise for that however and upped in grade now; not sure the drop in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Roscommon winner upped 10lb and goes up in grade; not sure the drop in trip will suit
runners.owner: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe

runners.name: Dynamic Force
