runners.spotlight: Speedy in his early days, winning three times over 5f for Robert Cowell; best recent form has been in 7f Down Royal handicaps, latest last Friday when strong-finishing fourth, having been keen; not sure drop back to 6f will suit.
runners.comment: Down Royal run last Friday encouraging but not sure drop back to 6f will suit

runners.name: Poweracclaim
runners.spotlight: Maiden showed promise in C&D maiden last year; handicap debut run in April was okay (staying on late after slow start) but didn't improve at Naas last month (both over 5f); return to 6f should suit and remains a potential improver.
runners.comment: Return to 6f should suit and remains a potential improver

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Won two for Owen Burrows in 2024 but no joy yet for this stable and hard to fancy despite plummeting mark
runners.comment: Long losing run for this stable and hard to fancy despite plummeting mark

runners.name: Salah Belle
runners.spotlight: Won over slightly further here two years ago but not since and back from a break after AW winter campaign; tongue-tie tried.
runners.comment: Won here two years ago; back from a break after AW winter campaign; tongue-tie tried

runners.name: Rosato
runners.spotlight: Three 6f wins came at Navan (twice) and last summer at Naas off 1lb higher; entitled to come on from C&D comeback run in April.
runners.comment: Won last summer at Naas off 1lb higher; entitled to come on from C&D comeback run

runners.name: Ukiyo
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Cork for Gavin Cromwell last year off 50 and couple of good runs this year, runner-up at Navan in March (6f, heavy) and most recently in first-time tongue-tie on quicker ground at Listowel on Sunday when staying on to chase home a clear-cut winner over 7f (good); one to consider.
runners.comment: Listowel second on Sunday has to be respected making quick return

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner is 0-5 on turf for current trainer this year, not beaten too far on penultimate; handicapper giving him every chance and new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Yet to hit form for this yard but handicapper giving him every chance; new headgear combo

runners.name: Lady Mary Heath
runners.spotlight: Couple of good runs here this term over further, most recently no luck in running when staying on third of 17 over 7f in April from a similar draw; not sure drop in trip will be in her favour.
runners.comment: Couple of good runs here this term over further, not sure drop in trip will suit

runners.name: Rappell
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, the latest over C&D in 2024 off 66; recent form well below his best however so needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: No turf win since C&D success in 2024; recent form well below his best

runners.name: Prime Sign
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced mare won over C&D over a year ago off this mark (good); absent since okay AW run in November  with cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: C&D winner a year ago off this mark; absent since okay AW run in November; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Dandy Land
runners.spotlight: Couple of hard-luck stories from three maiden runs last October; potential improver entering handicaps, but may need this.
runners.comment: Potential improver entering handicaps but may need this

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: Now 0-9 but has ability judged on his keeping-on fourth of 21 over C&D in April (soft); didn't get a clear run at Down Royal on Friday so remains of interest.
runners.comment: C&D fourth in April standout run; no lucking in running at Down Royal last Friday

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Fantasy Force
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with third of six in novice contest (5f, good) at the York Dante festival, having attracted a bit of support; the form pick and open to progress to boot.
runners.comment: Showed promise with third of six at the York Dante festival; the form pick

runners.name: Spectacular Diver
runners.spotlight: Finished behind a couple of today's rivals at York, failing to back up his Pontefract effort (second of four); effectively needs to prove he's capable of progressing.
runners.comment: Finished behind a couple of today's rivals at York last time

runners.name: Hidden Gift
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros yearling, £150,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; Sioux Nation half-sister to 5f 2yo winner New Theory (RPR 79); dam 7f scorer (72), sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Final Frontier, out of 5f 2yo Listed winner; another interesting newcomer for Wathnan Racing, whose strike-rate with 2yos is strong.
runners.comment: £150,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; another interesting newcomer for Wathnan Racing

runners.name: Social Symbol
runners.spotlight: $60,000 yearling, £145,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Violence; sibling to two dirt winners, notably Royal Spa (US 6f-8.5f including 2yo/Grade 2; RPR 116); dam US 5.5f-7f dirt scorer, half-sister to 7f 2yo Grade 1 winner Sippican Harbor; similar to Hidden Gift in terms of sales info; shortlisted.
runners.comment: £145,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; similar to Hidden Gift in terms of sales info

runners.name: Sole Ruler
runners.spotlight: Inns Of Court half-brother to prolific 5f/7f winner Elegant Erin (including 2yo; RPR 98); dam placed 6f (66), out of 9.4f/1m4f scorer; yard has modest record with 2yos.
runners.comment: Inns Of Court colt; yard has modest record with 2yos

runners.name: Calef
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form having trailed home last of four in Carlisle event (5f) won by a smart prospect; achieved little on the figures and needs to improve sharply.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form having finished last of four at Carlisle

runners.name: Dubai Champion
runners.spotlight: Modest fourth of six (about 5l behind Fantasy Force) in 5f contest at the York Dante festival, having started the rank outsider; perhaps one for nurseries later on.
runners.comment: Modest fourth (behind Fantasy Force) at York; one for nurseries later on
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

races.race_name: Book Online At ripon-races.co.uk Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gone By
runners.spotlight: Juddmonte Irish Oaks entrant; runner-up to stablemate A La Prochaine (since third in the Cheshire Oaks) on Newbury debut (1m, soft; 17-2) last October, and again forced to settle for second-best on seasonal reappearance at Ascot (1m2f; 10-11 favourite); this is a golden opportunity.
runners.comment: Shown clear promise in defeat at Newbury (1m) & Ascot (1m2f); this is a golden opportunity

runners.name: Golden Step
runners.spotlight: Sister to the 1m3f AW winner Alfred Spero; slowly away, and only a modest third to a long odds-on favourite on Kempton debut (1m3f fillies' novice; 5-1, RPR 54) four weeks ago; is entitled to have taken on board the lessons learned there, and switches to turf for second start.
runners.comment: Slowly away, and only a modest third to a long odds-on favourite on Kempton debut (1m3f).

runners.name: Joud
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns foal, and a half-sister to three winners; nothing special, third of four on Newcastle debut (6f novice; 5-1) six months ago; this longer trip should suit on pedigree but she has plenty to find on the limited evidence we've witnessed.
runners.comment: Nothing special, third of four on Newcastle debut (6f novice) six months ago; new trip.

runners.name: Wootton's Gal
runners.spotlight: Dam was a 1m4f-1m6f Flat/2m hurdle winner; slowly away and always behind on Thirsk debut (7f maiden fillies', good; 50-1) last July; by far the least likely candidate in this four-runner field.
runners.comment: Slowly away and always behind on Thirsk debut (7f maiden fillies', good; 50-1) last July.

runners.name: Amidst The Chaos
runners.spotlight: Denied a clear run when third over C&D (good) in April and he had the same issue when fourth of 13 at Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) three weeks ago; unproven on slow ground; could be bang there if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Denied a clear run when making frame on last two starts; bang there if getting the breaks

runners.name: Travis
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was admirably consistent in the second half of last year and reappeared with a good second at Newcastle in March; however, after a lesser run at Thirsk he was beaten a long way at Redcar only 16 days ago, and needs to leave that latest performance miles behind.
runners.comment: Good second on reappearance in March but beaten a long way 16 days ago

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: Knocking on the door prior to winning at Hamilton (8.3f, good) last month and again ran well when third at Redcar (7f, good to firm) nine days ago; also effective on good to soft ground; could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: He's been in good form since returning to action and could be in the shake-up

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Just three wins from his 47 starts, the latest back in June 2024; however, he was short-headed off this career-low mark at Hamilton (8.3f, good to firm) last Wednesday and could be in the mix once more; versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: Last win was back in June 2024 but short-headed off career-low mark at Hamilton last week

runners.name: The New Bay Pearl
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who failed to progress for Roger Varian and was soundly beaten at 100-1 on her stable and seasonal debut in April; upset in stalls and withdrawn at Redcar last month (66-1 at the time).
runners.comment: Failed to progress for Roger Varian and soundly beaten on her stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Avatar Jet
runners.spotlight: Made all on AW debut and second start back from a long absence at Southwell (1m, AW) in April, and third there since (1m) in a race that is working out very well; won on turf in 2024 and he's a strong candidate if his good recent form continues back on grass.
runners.comment: Won on Southwell AW in April and third there since in a race that has worked out very well

runners.name: Havana Touch
runners.spotlight: 5yo who was in top form on AW in the spring, with a win at Kempton (1m) being sandwiched by two seconds; showed some promise in May 2024 when he last raced on turf; key player if transferring good form back to grass.
runners.comment: In top form on AW in the spring and key player if that continues back on turf

runners.name: Superfortress
runners.spotlight: Didn't live up to market expectations on handicap debut in April (1m, AW), when he reared at the start, but this very lightly raced 5yo showed what he is capable of when second at Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) three weeks ago, when denied a clear run; unproven on slow ground; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 5yo; runner-up at Beverley on second handicap start; on the shortlist

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Beaten 19 times since winning in December 2024 on her second start; positive signs at the beginning of this campaign but she hasn't backed up those performances on her last two starts; may again be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Some positive signs to begin campaign but hasn't backed those up; losing spell continues

runners.name: Kiss Me My Love
runners.spotlight: She's worked her way back into form and last month's second-place finish at Thirsk (1m, good to firm) was followed by a win at Redcar (1m, good); won on soft ground last September; respected up just 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Won by a nose at Redcar last month and she's respected up just 3lb

runners.name: The Childe Of Hale
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) last July; three lesser runs followed and no return to form on his reappearance in April; he could improve for that latest effort but his claims aren't compelling.
runners.comment: Won off this mark last July but needs to take sizeable step forward from reappearance 9th
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (1m, AW) last October and he's run some good races on turf, but remains winless on grass after seven attempts and needs to produce more than he's managed on turf across his four outings in the spring.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle last October but hasn't performed at the same level this spring

runners.name: Jesmond Dawn
runners.spotlight: Below par on his last few starts in the winter but a break may have helped and he's on a handy mark on form as recent as last November; he's capable of running well when fresh and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Below par on last few starts in the winter but break may have helped and on a handy mark

runners.name: Eeetee
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Redcar (1m, good to firm/good to soft) last summer, the latest off today's mark; however, it was a tame end to the season and this 8yo needs to get back on track on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Won off this mark last July but tame end to season; needs to get back on track on return
runners.owner: Peter Clarke Racing And John Wainwright

runners.name: Glory Hyde
runners.spotlight: Didn't get the breaks at Beverley (5f, good to firm) on penultimate start, but she pounced late to continue her consistent spell at Redcar (6f, good; from 3lb lower) subsequently, justifying favouritism; is well drawn in stall 12, so should have a positive role to play.
runners.comment: Pounced late to continue consistent spell, justifying favouritism at Redcar (6f); up 3lb.

runners.name: Magic Boy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to an odds-on favourite in a C&D maiden (40-1) last June and mostly competitive since; made the breakthrough in a tight finish at Redcar (7f, Class 5, good; 28-1) last November; 2lb higher for seasonal reappearance, representing an in-form trainer.
runners.comment: Made breakthrough in tight finish at Redcar (7f; Class 5; 28-1) last November; 2lb higher.

runners.name: Men Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Formerly with Andrew Slattery, finishing a close fourth to this year's Irish Lincolnshire winner Ribee at Gowran Park (7f, heavy) last October; joined Ruth Carr soon after for 12,000gns; weakened quickly, ninth of 12 to Novello Lad at Doncaster (6f) 18 days ago; now 2lb lower.
runners.comment: Weakened quickly, ninth of 12 to Novello Lad at Doncaster (6f) 18 days ago; 2lb lower.
runners.owner: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Keldeo
runners.spotlight: Notched her third Catterick win, fending off Moostar there (6f, good; from 2lb lower) in early April; dropped back to 5f, she has twice finished fourth since, beaten nearly 7l at Carlisle over a fortnight ago; rain would be no major concern, but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Twice fourth since winning at Catterick (6f; from 2lb lower) in April; others appeal more.
runners.owner: Ownaracehorse, O Pears and T&T Racing Ltd

runners.name: Beattie Is Back
runners.spotlight: 4-29; runner-up off this mark at Wolverhampton (7f; beaten a neck) in February; in rear throughout at York (6f; 40-1) on return from a three-month break; this is slightly easier (Class 5) and Ethan Tindall takes off a handy 5lb.
runners.comment: In rear throughout at York (6f; 40-1) on return from a three-month break; this is easier.
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Fortamour
runners.spotlight: Six-time C&D winner; easing mark and back to form, just held by U Sure Do over C&D (good; 25-1) under four weeks ago; is capable of handling easier ground, and well drawn close to the stands' rail in stall 10; stablemate of William Dewhirst.
runners.comment: Six-time C&D winner; easing mark and back to form, just held over C&D (good; 25-1) latest.

runners.name: Blazing Son
runners.spotlight: More productive on AW (5-17) than on turf (2-28) and was only run out of it inside the final furlong at Newcastle (7f Class 5; 9-1) in March; less effective since (0-2) but is slowly easing in the weights, and is perhaps best suited by 6f.
runners.comment: More productive on AW (5-17) than on turf (2-28); first run over 6f since last July.

runners.name: Miss Willows
runners.spotlight: Made all, scrambling home at Redcar (7f, Class 6, good to soft; from 3lb lower) last September; showed that same speed before weakening on seasonal reappearance at Thirsk (7f, good; 11th of 14; 25-1) in April; has a tricky draw to navigate, unless electing to go far side.
runners.comment: Weakened on seasonal reappearance at Thirsk (7f, good; 11th of 14) in April; tricky draw.

runners.name: Cairdeas
runners.spotlight: Ex William Haggas; no win since his juvenile days, but has twice ran well over this C&D, including when fourth (from 4lb higher) last August; no match for stablemate Novello Lad at Doncaster (6f, good to soft; 25-1) recently but that comeback run ought to have him spot on for this.
runners.comment: Runner-up to stablemate Novello Lad at Doncaster (6f, good to soft; 25-1) recently; chance

runners.name: William Dewhirst
runners.spotlight: Regular on AW at Newcastle, his last six wins (6f-7f) coming there, most of those under Paul Mulrennan; has struggled to recapture his form in 2026, trailing home ninth of ten at Carlisle (7f, soft; 22-1) 16 days ago; stablemate Fortamour makes way more appeal.
runners.comment: Struggled to recapture form in 2026, trailing home ninth at Carlisle (7f, soft) latest.

runners.name: Bay Breeze
runners.spotlight: Half of his wins have come over C&D and he finished sixth (Fortamour third) in this race in 2024; runner-up in added blinkers at Hamilton (6f, soft; 11-2) last September, but has failed to sparkle since (0-3); easing mark and a return to Ripon are the positives.
runners.comment: Runner-up in added blinkers at Hamilton (6f, soft) last September; modest since (0-3).

runners.name: Blind Beggar
runners.spotlight: Has won just once since April 2024, at Sandown (5f, good; from 6lb higher) last July; joined this trainer soon after for 15,000gns, and runner-up on stable debut; nowhere near his best in three starts this year, including when twelfth of 20 at York (6f; 50-1) latest.
runners.comment: Nowhere near best in three starts this year, incl when twelfth of 20 at York (6f) latest.
runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: Pushed out under hands and heels to score at Musselburgh (5f, good; from 4lb lower) last month, but was no match for stablemate Marajito over C&D (good, 9-2) last week; remains in fine fettle though, and has a C&D win to her name, so she ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: No match for stablemate Marajito over C&D (good, 9-2) last week; remains in fine fettle.

runners.name: Speeding Bullet
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark at Pontefract (6f, good; 28-1) last June and finished his season with a pair of second placings at Southwell (6f) and Haydock (5f); low key so far this term, including when seventh (6.5l behind Wen Moon) at Carlisle latest; stablemate of Rock Of England.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Pontefract (6f, good; 28-1) last June; low key so far this term (0-2)
runners.owner: Slaters Arms Racing And Ged Shields

runners.name: Wen Moon
runners.spotlight: Poorly drawn and well beaten on seasonal reappearance here (6f) in April, but much more competitive when third at Carlisle (5f, good; 9-2) 16 days ago; bypassed an option at Catterick on Saturday (unsuitable ground) in favour of this; rain - three wins on soft - would aid his cause.
runners.comment: Third at Carlisle (5f, good) 16 days ago; rain - three wins on soft - would aid his cause.
runners.owner: Mps Racing, D Shapiro & D Clifford

runners.name: Betweenthesticks
runners.spotlight: Kicked off the season with back-to-back 5f wins at Wolverhampton and Pontefract before being run down by Vince L'Amour over C&D (Hurt You Never third) next time; nowhere near those same levels at Pontefract (ninth of ten) latest, so needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Run down late over C&D (Hurt You Never third) penultimate start; poor at Pontefract since.
runners.owner: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr

runners.name: Rock Of England
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways on AW at Newcastle (5f; beating stablemate Herakles) in April; ran well from this revised mark at Pontefract (5f; third, reportedly bled from the nose) subsequently; trainer won this in 2021, 2022, and 2024, so he's one to have on the shortlist.
runners.comment: AW winner in April; ran well from this revised mark at Pontefract (third) latest; player.

runners.name: Azuinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Won three last year, including at Ayr (5f, good to soft; 13-8 favourite) in July; yet to threaten this year, restless in the stalls at Musselburgh (5f) in April; easing mark - sub 70 for first time in a while - helpful, but tough to recommend at present.
runners.comment: Yet to threaten this year; easing mark - sub 70 - helpful; tough to recommend at present.
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: High Opinion
runners.spotlight: Shaped as if in need of comeback run at Nottingham (5f, good; sixth of eight) in April; has some decent C&D form to his name and is 10lb lower (and claimer ridden) than when seeing off Our Absent Friends here in August 2024; foolish to discount hastily.
runners.comment: 10lb lower (and claimer ridden) than when seeing off Our Absent Friends here in Aug 2024.

runners.name: Hurt You Never
runners.spotlight: 14-66 and somewhat of a Hamilton Park specialist; creditable third to Vince L'Amour (Betweenthesticks second) over C&D on penultimate start but, that aside, she hasn't been overly competitive; easing mark (8lb below her last winning one) offers some hope.
runners.comment: Creditable third (Betweenthesticks second) over C&D penultimate; less effective otherwise.

runners.name: Popty Ping
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW and overall record is just 1-18, the success in March; however, she continues in form and wasn't disgraced in most recent turf attempt; frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Record is only 1-18 but she continues in form; again has frame possibilities

runners.name: Ziggy's Avenger
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to showing improvement with keeping-on third at Kempton (1m, AW) on handicap debut last time; may build on that effort and take another step forward; respected off same mark.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to improved effort at Kempton on handicap debut last time; respected
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Harry Kassap Llc

runners.name: Karakula Dancer
runners.spotlight: Consistent record, mostly on AW, this year features three wins; posted a rare unplaced effort last time (behind Ziggy's Avenger) but shaped as if still in form; ran well on turf two starts ago; solid.
runners.comment: Consistent sort, mostly on AW; ran well on turf two starts ago; solid chance

runners.name: What Fools Believe
runners.spotlight: Opened her account in 1m AW handicap at Southwell in April; has failed to back up that form in two starts since, latest switched to turf; something to prove.
runners.comment: Has failed to back up the form of her Southwell AW win

runners.name: Deep Sleep
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; best form when fourth on two occasions, including at Hamilton (upped to 1m; handicap debut) latest start; again has frame possibilities but needs progress to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Again has frame possibilities but needs progress to get off the mark

runners.name: Talking In Kode
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who fared best of the rest behind 2-13 shot in weak maiden at Beverley (7.5f) most recently; that form isn't hugely convincing but it gives her something to possibly build on.
runners.comment: Latest effort gives her something to possibly build on; handicap debut

runners.name: Cobalt Comet
runners.spotlight: Has posted third-place finishes over 1m at Southwell (AW) and Carlisle (soft) since switched to handicaps; has place claims as things stand and wouldn't need to improve much to open his account.
runners.comment: Couple of third-place finishes since switched to handicaps; in the mix

runners.name: Paroda Diva
runners.spotlight: Proved suited by the step up to 1m at Haydock (good to soft) last time, recording a first win since joining her current yard and belatedly doubling her tally; unexposed at this trip; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Suited by the step up to 1m at Haydock last time, belatedly doubling her tally

runners.name: Louis Veron
runners.spotlight: Close second in 15-runner Chepstow novice (2m, good to soft) on debut in October; absent until turned over at skinny odds at Southwell (2m, good) last month but the winner was a useful Flat performer who has since followed up; ought to go one better here.
runners.comment: Runner-up on both hurdling starts and has good chance to go one better here

runners.name: Garde My Guinness
runners.spotlight: Third in three good-ground bumpers in early part of last season but the form isn't anything to get excited about, and she was soundly beaten on her first two hurdling starts, the latter last month, after a layoff.
runners.comment: Third in three run-of-the-mill bumpers last year but soundly beaten on both hurdle starts

runners.name: Dunstall Star
runners.spotlight: Led close home to win modest Exeter bumper (good ground) in April and made a lot of the running over C&D on last month's hurdling debut, albeit he was held in third before last-flight fall; his tendency to race too freely is a worry but he has a good chance to collect at least a minor prize.
runners.comment: Made a lot of the running before last-flight fall over C&D on last month's hurdle debut

runners.name: Colibri Bleu
runners.spotlight: Won a two-runner maiden point in March 2025 but failed to complete his next five points; makes very limited appeal on rules debut.
runners.comment: Won two-runner point in 2025 but failed to complete next five points; rules debut

runners.name: Connemara Joe
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all seven Irish points, most recently when pulled up on soft ground in April; can only be watched on this rules debut.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all seven Irish points; can only be watched on rules debut

runners.name: Lyric
runners.spotlight: Showed some useful Flat form for the Gosden stable in 2024 but last year's stable debut was disappointing and he made a low-key start to his hurdling career when eighth of ten in Musselburgh maiden (2m1f, good to soft; jumping lacked fluency) in March; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat in 2024 but made a low-key stable debut in March

runners.name: Chambers
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability on the Flat (albeit without being placed) and wasn't disgraced when sixth on last month's hurdle debut at Fontwell (2m1f, good) but was well beaten after racing too freely at Bangor 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on last month's hurdling debut but well beaten since; look elsewhere

runners.name: Tyson
runners.spotlight: Started off for Dan Skelton with some disappointing efforts but comfortably drew 10l clear at Huntingdon (2m4f, good to firm) nine days ago and has now won two of his last three; probably still well treated under 7lb penalty here and won't mind if the ground softens; hard to beat if stamina holds out on first venture beyond 2m6f.
runners.comment: Carries 7lb penalty for comfortable 2m4f win nine days ago; tries a new trip; big player

runners.name: Baltray
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his maiden hurdle win in 2023 but fairly lightly raced in the meantime and ran quite well when third at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last month; still unexposed over this sort of trip; raced mainly on good ground; could have a say.
runners.comment: Placed at Newton Abbot (3m2f) last month and remains unexposed over this sort of trip

runners.name: Spitalfield
runners.spotlight: Ended string of heavy defeats when third in first-time cheekpieces here (3m1f, good; handes soft) last month but didn't find much in the closing stages, and it's hard to tell whether he'll build on that performance.
runners.comment: Latest run was big step back in right direction but he remains hard to predict

runners.name: Luna Run
runners.spotlight: Just 1-16 over hurdles when trained by Evan Williams and was unable to justify odds-on favouritism when only sixth of 12 here (2m5f, good) on last month's stable debut; may yet come good for Harry Derham, though, and this move back up in trip will suit; wears refitted tongue-tie; gained her win on good to firm but has form on good to soft; 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: Only sixth when odds-on for stable debut but may yet come good for Harry Derham

runners.name: Broadway Honey
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 28l when fifth of six here (3m1f, good) last month and is now 0-6 over hurdles; seemed to be travelling okay in the lead before bad jumping error knocked the stuffing out of him that day and remains unexposed over this sort of trip but, nevertheless, others have stronger claims; raced only on good/good to soft ground.
runners.comment: Beaten about 28l over 3m1f here last month and now 0-6 over hurdles

runners.name: Groom De Cotte
runners.spotlight: Won over fences 13 months ago but returned from layoff with lacklustre hurdle run in April and is difficult to enthuse over after that performance; has usual tongue-tie refitted; seems versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: Won a chase last year but returned from layoff with lacklustre hurdle run in April

runners.name: Touchwoodexpress
runners.spotlight: Absent since two below-par runs in November but returned from a break to win five-runner contest at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) in September, and is now back on the same mark; seems best on good ground but once won a point on soft; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Absent since November but won off today's mark when fresh in September can't rule out

runners.name: Walkinthecotswolds
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb lower than when making all over hurdles at Chepstow (2m3f, heavy) in early 2025 but has poor completion record in the meantime; pulled up soon after bad jumping error in early stages of a recent chase and now reverts to hurdling; the forecast rain will aid her cause but her stamina is unproven and she can't be supported with much confidence.
runners.comment: Will be aided by rain but has poor completion record since her win in early 2025

runners.name: Hitthekettle
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all nine hurdling starts; gave fairly good account before weakening into fourth of seven over C&D (good) in March but is effectively 9lb higher from out of the weights today; gained his point win on soft to heavy ground.
runners.comment: Ran quite well when fourth over C&D last time but is effectively 9lb higher today

runners.name: Midnight View
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score over C&D (good) in April, his ninth start over hurdles; still unexposed over this sort of trip but was a shade disappointing when well-held third at Ludlow (2m5f, good) last month and probably won't be favoured by any rain.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April; not quite in same form last time; rain a likely negative

runners.name: Amatchmadeinheaven
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who won under owner-jockey Amber Jackson-Fennell on hurdle debut at Exeter (2m, good to soft) in March; below that form over 2m5f in April (raced too freely) but remains open to improvement on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won on hurdle debut in March (2m); below that form next time (2m5f) but remains unexposed

runners.name: Dream's Ka
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles at seventh attempt, with ready success at Bangor (2m3f, good; has been placed on good to soft) last month; seemed to have a lot left in the tank that day and commands respect here, despite subsequent 12lb rise.
runners.comment: Won with a good deal left in the tank at Bangor last month; 12lb higher here

runners.name: Kentucky River
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who won easily over hurdles at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in April, his second handicap; well beaten in valuable Fakenham contest since but back in calmer waters and on a more conventional track here; remains of interest, albeit the forecast rain may not be in his favour.
runners.comment: Well beaten in valuable race last time but won easily two starts ago; remains of interest
runners.owner: J R B Williams & Emdells Limited

runners.name: Modern Style
runners.spotlight: Launched handicap career with two comfortable wins on good ground (2m/2m3f; unraced on ground any slower) in April, and ran well in defeat at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last month; back up in trip here and probably still on the upgrade; must be considered.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo who is 2-3 in handicaps; back up in trip here; must be considered

runners.name: Carpe Diem
runners.spotlight: Has continued to run well since his Huntingdon win in Februaray (2m4f, soft), last time giving everything in a narrow defeat over C&D; 2lb higher with Tom Hutsby again aboard; should have every chance.
runners.comment: Continued his good run of form when narrowly beaten over C&D last month

runners.name: Thickthorn Tom
runners.spotlight: Better for this headgear combination, following his second at Fontwell with a narrow but comfortable success at Newton Abbot last week (2m5f, good) for which he carries a penalty; sure to go well over a trip he stays.
runners.comment: Narrow but comfortable winner last week for which he carries a penalty; the one to beat

runners.name: King Of The Story
runners.spotlight: All four chase wins have come over shorter distances but this far is worth a go; ran quite well after a break at Fontwell and when beaten 27l into second here last time he was going well when clouting four out.
runners.comment: When beaten 27l into second here last time he was going well when clouting four out

runners.name: Pescatorius
runners.spotlight: Left Olly Murphy for 5,500gns and pretty much tailed off in four handicap chases since.
runners.comment: Left Olly Murphy for 5,500gns and pretty much tailed off in four handicap chases since

runners.name: Knightsbridge
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but was close up in a 2m3f handicap off 5lb higher; ran okay when 8l off the winner on chase debut here in March (2m4f, good) and worth another go at 3m.
runners.comment: Only 8l off the winner on chase debut here in March (2m4f) and worth another go at 3m

runners.name: Misteroddsocks
runners.spotlight: Back from wind surgery when winning narrowly at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) but well beaten off this 5lb higher mark back there, finishing behind Thickthorn Tom; unraced beyond 2m5f.
runners.comment: Well beaten off this mark last time, finishing behind Thickthorn Tom

runners.name: Northern Symphonie
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner but below her best in that discipline for current yard and again when a remote third on chase debut at Ludlow (2m4f, good; first-time cheekpieces after wind surgery); others preferred.
runners.comment: Not at her best since moving yards; third on chase debut was remote

runners.name: Siorai
runners.spotlight: Two seconds before a respectable run in a quite valuable race on handicap debut at Sandown in April (2m, good); she's up to winning races off this mark; hood fitted for last three races is now removed.
runners.comment: Respectable run in a quite valuable race on handicap debut at Sandown in April

runners.name: Best Night
runners.spotlight: Won at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March but subsequent efforts have lacked the same dash; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March but subsequent efforts have lacked the same dash

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: In a first-time tongue-tie when winning easily on last week's chase debut at Worcester (2m, good) and overcame a scare at the last flight to comfortably exploit the same mark back over hurdles at Southwell on Tuesday; carries a penalty but looks very well in.
runners.comment: Hosed up back over hurdles yesterday and looks very well in under a penalty

runners.name: Granny B
runners.spotlight: Flat winner but 0-17 over hurdles and her third two runs ago was remote; opposable.

runners.name: Meowth
runners.spotlight: Made the frame five consecutive times over C&D in January-April and last month's sixth from stall 10 here was perfectly respectable; recent 2lb drop helps; ought to be right in the thick of things.
runners.comment: In frame five consecutive times this year and didn't run badly from tough draw last time

runners.name: The Heir
runners.spotlight: In good form in recent months and was nicely in command in closing stages over C&D a fortnight ago; recent 7lb rise demands more from him but he's well drawn again; respected.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for recent C&D win but was nicely in command close home and is well drawn again

runners.name: Bright Day
runners.spotlight: Scored over 7f at Sha Tin in early part of this season and, having dipped 1lb below that mark, exploited favourable draw over C&D a week ago; evidently in good nick but 7lb rise is probably enough to prevent a follow up.
runners.comment: C&D winner from stall 1 a fortnight ago; 7lb rise probably enough to prevent a follow up

runners.name: Telecom Fighters
runners.spotlight: Reserve; belied triple-digit odds when fourth from stall 11 over C&D last month but was quickly back in the doldrums here a week later.
runners.comment: Fourth at big odds here last month but quickly back in the doldrums a week later

runners.name: Power Koepp
runners.spotlight: Not seen to best effect on rare dirt start last time out and no longer the force of old, but was running fairly well over C&D in March/April and has been given a chance by the handicapper; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Not seen to best effect on dirt last time but returns to turf on attractive mark
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Simon Ng Kar Ming

runners.name: Ace Champion
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong but went close over C&D in March and last month's keeping-on sixth at Sha Tin was also a good effort; no surprise if he features from stall 1.
runners.comment: 0-6 in Hong Kong but went close over C&D in March and has the inside draw again

runners.name: Amazing Kid
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected four C&D wins this season; not disgraced from wide stalls here on last two starts and has more favourable berth today; likely contender.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins this season; not disgraced from wide stalls here on last two starts

runners.name: Igor Stravinsky
runners.spotlight: Recorded narrow win on dirt when dipping into Class 4 in April but has been soundly beaten twice since returned to this grade; drops back to more suitable trip after attempting 1m last time but will probably remain vulnerable at this level.
runners.comment: Narrow Class 4 winner in April but soundly beaten in this grade twice since
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Cyrus Li Kin Wai

runners.name: Tactical Ace
runners.spotlight: 2-5 when based in Australia but unable to land a significant blow on first three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f) and has a double-digit draw.
runners.comment: Unable to land a significant blow on first three HK starts and has double-digit draw

runners.name: King Profit
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over 6f before winning readily over 5f in April; moves up in grade today but further progress is possible; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Upped in grade since ready 5f win here in April but further progress is possible
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs William Y W Lai, Stanley Y C Hong & Sammy Y C Long

runners.name: Harmony N Blessed
runners.spotlight: Regressive 9yo; suggested he might be ready to capitalise upon some help from the handicapper when second over C&D in April, but a down-the-field run followed and he's drawn wide today.
runners.comment: Second over C&D in April but a down-the-field run followed and he's drawn wide today
runners.owner: Cheung Ming Man, Cheung Ying Ho Et Al

runners.name: Super Joy N Fun
runners.spotlight: Seems to have turned a corner on last two appearances, finishing fourth on turf and dirt, but his outside draw is an obvious negative today.
runners.comment: Seems to have turned a corner on last two appearances but has the outside stall today
runners.owner: Johnny Wong Chun Nam, Nancy Wong Et Al

runners.name: Symbol Of Strength
runners.spotlight: In good form on turf this year and last month's dirt defeat is not viewed harshly; holds no secrets from the handicapper but has each-way claims from stall 4.
runners.comment: Hold no secrets from the handicapper but has each-way claims from stall 4

runners.name: Greater Bae
runners.spotlight: Reserve; didn't fire here last time but won in pretty good style over C&D in January and remains lightly raced in Hong Kong; contender if first-time blinkers give him a boost.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but has won over C&D this year and is still lightly raced in HK

runners.name: Jubilant Winner
runners.spotlight: Four wins this season, the latest gained from the front over C&D in April; never looked dangerous when ridden patiently from a wide stall here last month but can revert to positive tactics from stall 3 today.
runners.comment: Four wins this season; stymied by wide draw last time; can revert to positive tactics now

runners.name: Happy Index
runners.spotlight: Placed twice at Sha Tin since winning there in January and had tough draw when midfield over C&D last month; not ideally berthed today either but not yet fully exposed in Hong Kong; a possible.
runners.comment: Placed twice at Sha Tin since winning there in January; had tough draw here last time

runners.name: Dancing Classics
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who won twice in Class 4 last year (5f/6f); absent since running poorly over C&D in this grade in November, though, and returns with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Won twice in Class 4 in 2025; absent since running poorly in this grade in November

runners.name: Pocketing
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Group 2 in Australia last April; made fairly encouraging Hong Kong debut over 7f in March and may not have been suited by soft ground when well beaten over 1m last month; ought to be capable of better.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner in Australia; has made quiet start to HK career but can do better

runners.name: Beauty Alliance
runners.spotlight: In career-best form in recent weeks, winning narrowly over 1m here in April before finishing third behind Packing Angel last month; fine over today's longer trip; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: In career-best form in recent weeks and has each-way claims at least

runners.name: Silvery Breeze
runners.spotlight: Improving 5yo who pipped a next-time-out winner over 1m here in April and probably ended up with too much to do when running-on fourth behind Packing Angel here (1m) three weeks ago; could have untapped potential over his extra furlong; high on the list.
runners.comment: Two wins this season; ended up with too much to do when fourth last time; still improving

runners.name: Joy Of Spring
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in December and was placed on consecutive appearances at Sha Tin (1m/1m1f) in March/April, but has suffered a dip in form lately and has a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Placed twice in March/April but has suffered a dip in form lately
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Liveandletlive
runners.spotlight: Consistent 5yo who deserved his C&D win three weeks ago (made all); 7lb higher and not so well drawn today, though, and others in this field look stronger.
runners.comment: Deserved last month's C&D win (made all) but might be vulnerable after 7lb rise

runners.name: Armor Golden Eagle
runners.spotlight: Scored in good style over 1m last week and has now won three of his last four; upped in trip today; needs to be taken very seriously in current mood.
runners.comment: Scored in good style over 1m last week and has now won three of his last four

runners.name: Californiatotality
runners.spotlight: Now 2lb lower than when going very close over C&D in October, but recent efforts have been disappointing and he needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Recent efforts have been disappointing and he needs to turn a corner

runners.name: Packing Angel
runners.spotlight: Close third in last season's Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) and looked back in prime form when scoring over 1m here three weeks ago; may yet have a bigger run in him; respected.
runners.comment: Back in prime form over 1m here last month and may yet have a bigger run in him

runners.name: Awesome Fluke
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner this season (1m1f/1m); still in good form but recent efforts suggest he has very little margin for error off current mark.
runners.comment: In good form but appears to have very little margin for error off current mark

runners.name: Helene Feeling
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two course wins (1m1f/1m) and has held his form pretty well since; has edged back down to his last winning mark and Ellis Wong claims 3lb today; ought to be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two course wins and has edged his way back down to a good mark now

runners.name: Soleil Fighter
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D in February and ran at least at well when second to Packing Angel here (1m) three weeks ago; remains on a tough mark but is well drawn to attack again.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D in February and ran big race in defeat over C&D last month

runners.name: Another Zonda
runners.spotlight: Just held on for Class 4 C&D win in April, but was safely held off today's mark last month and moves back up in grade today; others preferred.
runners.comment: Held off this mark since narrow C&D win in April and moves back up in grade today

runners.name: Keefy
runners.spotlight: Has a point to prove after last month's below-par effort (1m1f) but was consistent beforehand and has slipped to a good mark; big player if back on song.
runners.comment: Below form last time but largely consistent this year and on a good mark now

runners.name: Stormi
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; won three in a row over C&D in Class 4 at start of this year but has not been in quite the same form lately; favourable draw not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Began year with three C&D wins; not quite in same form lately

runners.name: Lucky My Way
runners.spotlight: Reserve; out of form over 7f for Manfred Man this season and made low-key stable debut over 6f last month; will be trying a new trip if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Out of form over 6f/7f this season; moves up in trip today
runners.owner: Patrick Wong Ho Yin, Ricky Choy Wing Kay Et Al

runners.name: Casa Rochester
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D in early part of this season and returned from break with very respectable fifth here a fortnight ago; usually ridden patiently; could have a say if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Returned from break with respectable run a fortnight ago; could have a say

runners.name: California Moxie
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 this season but has found things tougher since upped to this grade in March, albeit he kept on well for fourth here two starts ago.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but finding things tougher since upped in grade

runners.name: Yee Cheong Glory
runners.spotlight: Gained both wins over 6f in Class 4; moves up to 1m after two respectable 7f runs from wide stalls last month but has another tough draw; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Upped to 1m after two respectable 7f runs last month but has another tough draw
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Yu So Fong

runners.name: Seward
runners.spotlight: Reserve; signed off in Ireland with two 7f wins in April 2025 but was soundly beaten on his first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m).
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on his first two Hong Kong starts
runners.owner: David Philip Boehm & Anthony Cheung Hin Shun

runners.name: All Round Winner
runners.spotlight: Came from the rear to win over C&D in April and was placed off new mark last month; unable to land a telling blow here on latest outing but has possibilities if this race is run to suit.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark since win from off the pace in April; a possible if race run to suit
runners.owner: Clement Chan Kam Wing, Maggie Ho Sze Nga Et Al

runners.name: Class
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; well down the field on all four Hong Kong starts (7f-1m); sports refitted visor and first-time tongue-tie today.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo; well down the field on all four Hong Kong starts
runners.owner: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho

runners.name: Fivefortwo
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season and took another step forward when running-on second over C&D three weeks ago; may still have a bigger run in him; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins this season; latest C&D second was another step forward; in the mix

runners.name: Smart Avenue
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who bounced back from a disappointing effort when winning readily in first-time visor at Sha Tin (1m) last month; another 7lb higher here but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Quickly back on track when winning readily in new visor at Sha Tin last month; respected
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Chan Chung Yin & Mr & Mrs Clevin Got Chong Key

runners.name: Mister Dapper
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing 6yo who posted career-best performance when close second at Sha Tin in April; didn't fire last time, though, and needs to find hidden reserves to win here.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time and current mark demands a career-best performance
runners.owner: P Chan Pak Hou, J Chan Chi Kwong & J Chan Chon San

runners.name: Without Compare
runners.spotlight: Held on gamely to open Hong Kong account under enterprising ride here in March, his fifth attempt, and posted another good effort when second at Sha Tin last month; drawn wide today but still considered.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Sha Tin since opening HK account over C&D in March; one to consider

runners.name: Jimmy P
runners.owner: Madaket Stables LLC, Molly Willis & Paul Willis
